cubes.name: Appointments

measures.name: Appointments.count
measures.title: Appointments Count
measures.description: Total number of current appointment service segments

meta.label: Appointment count
meta.tooltip: Total number of appointment service segments.

measures.name: Appointments.reservation_count
measures.title: Appointments Reservation Count
measures.description: Number of distinct appointment reservations

meta.label: Reservations
meta.tooltip: Number of distinct appointment reservations.

measures.name: Appointments.accepted_count
measures.title: Appointments Accepted Count
measures.description: Number of accepted appointment service segments

measures.name: Appointments.pending_count
measures.title: Appointments Pending Count
measures.description: Number of pending appointment service segments

measures.name: Appointments.no_show_count
measures.title: Appointments No Show Count
measures.description: Number of appointment service segments marked as no-shows

measures.name: Appointments.cancelled_count
measures.title: Appointments Cancelled Count
measures.description: Number of appointment service segments cancelled by the customer, buyer, or seller

measures.name: Appointments.no_show_reservation_count
measures.title: Appointments No Show Reservation Count
measures.description: Number of distinct reservations marked as no-shows. Use this instead of no_show_count for booking-level reporting, since a single reservation can have multiple service segments.

meta.label: No-shows
meta.tooltip: Number of appointment bookings marked as no-shows.

measures.name: Appointments.cancelled_reservation_count
measures.title: Appointments Cancelled Reservation Count
measures.description: Number of distinct reservations cancelled by the customer, buyer, or seller. Use this instead of cancelled_count for booking-level reporting, since a single reservation can have multiple service segments.

meta.label: Cancellations
meta.tooltip: Number of appointment bookings cancelled by the customer, buyer, or seller.

measures.name: Appointments.total_duration_hours
measures.title: Appointments Total Duration Hours
measures.description: Total scheduled appointment duration in hours

measures.name: Appointments.avg_duration_minutes
measures.title: Appointments Avg Duration Minutes
measures.description: Average scheduled appointment service duration in minutes, across appointment service segments. Reflects the booked service length, not measured elapsed time.

meta.label: Average duration
meta.tooltip: Average scheduled length of an appointment service.

dimensions.name: Appointments.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Appointments Merchant Id
dimensions.description: The merchant ID that owns this appointment

dimensions.name: Appointments.location_id
dimensions.title: Appointments Location Id
dimensions.description: The location ID where this appointment is scheduled

dimensions.name: Appointments.location_name
dimensions.title: Appointments Location Name
dimensions.description: Display name of the location where this appointment is scheduled

dimensions.name: Appointments.reservation_id
dimensions.title: Appointments Reservation Id
dimensions.description: Identifier for the appointment reservation

dimensions.name: Appointments.status
dimensions.title: Appointments Status
dimensions.description: Current appointment reservation status from the appointments service

meta.label: Appointment status
meta.tooltip: Current status of the appointment booking.
meta.context: Appointment status is reported by the appointments service. Active appointments are usually pending or accepted. Cancelled, declined, and no-show appointments are retained as status values for analysis.

dimensions.name: Appointments.team_member_id
dimensions.title: Appointments Team Member Id
dimensions.description: Team member ID assigned to the appointment

dimensions.name: Appointments.team_member_name
dimensions.title: Appointments Team Member Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member assigned to the appointment

dimensions.name: Appointments.item_id
dimensions.title: Appointments Item Id
dimensions.description: Catalog item ID for the appointment service

dimensions.name: Appointments.item_name
dimensions.title: Appointments Item Name
dimensions.description: Catalog item name for the appointment service

dimensions.name: Appointments.item_variation_id
dimensions.title: Appointments Item Variation Id
dimensions.description: Catalog item variation ID for the appointment service variation

dimensions.name: Appointments.item_variation_name
dimensions.title: Appointments Item Variation Name
dimensions.description: Catalog item variation name for the appointment service variation

dimensions.name: Appointments.customer_token
dimensions.title: Appointments Customer Token
dimensions.description: Customer token associated with the appointment

dimensions.name: Appointments.starts_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Starts at
dimensions.description: Appointment start timestamp in UTC

dimensions.name: Appointments.ends_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Ends at
dimensions.description: Appointment end timestamp in UTC

dimensions.name: Appointments.reservation_created_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Reservation Created at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the reservation was created

dimensions.name: Appointments.reservation_updated_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Reservation Updated at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the reservation was last updated

dimensions.name: Appointments.reservation_booked_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Reservation Booked at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the reservation was booked

dimensions.name: Appointments.ordinal
dimensions.title: Appointments Ordinal
dimensions.description: Service ordinal within the reservation

dimensions.name: Appointments.recurring_instance_ordinal
dimensions.title: Appointments Recurring Instance Ordinal
dimensions.description: Ordinal for this instance within a recurring appointment series

dimensions.name: Appointments.is_recurring_reservation
dimensions.title: Appointments Is Recurring Reservation
dimensions.description: Whether this appointment segment is part of a recurring reservation series

dimensions.name: Appointments.needs_reingestion
dimensions.title: Appointments Needs Reingestion
dimensions.description: Whether the producer marked this recurring appointment series for future reingestion

dimensions.name: Appointments.line_item_uid
dimensions.title: Appointments Line Item Uid
dimensions.description: Line item UID associated with this appointment service segment

dimensions.name: Appointments.emitted_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Emitted at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the appointment reporting event was emitted by the producer

dimensions.name: Appointments.event_timestamp
dimensions.title: Appointments Event Timestamp
dimensions.description: CloudEvents timestamp attached to the data lake event

dimensions.name: Appointments.received_at
dimensions.title: Appointments Received at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the archival data lake pipeline received this event

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_timestamp
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_date
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_month
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_day
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_hour
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_minute
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Appointments.local_start_year
dimensions.title: Appointments Local Start Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

segments.name: Appointments.active
segments.title: Appointments Active
segments.description: Only active appointments with pending or accepted status

segments.name: Appointments.accepted
segments.title: Appointments Accepted
segments.description: Only accepted appointments

segments.name: Appointments.no_show
segments.title: Appointments No Show
segments.description: Only appointments marked as no-shows

segments.name: Appointments.recurring
segments.title: Appointments Recurring
segments.description: Only appointment segments that are part of a recurring reservation series

cubes.title: Appointments

meta.label: Appointments
meta.tooltip: Appointment bookings, status, staff, services, and duration

cubes.description: Appointment reservation segments from the appointments reporting data lake feed. Each row represents the latest non-deleted segment for a reservation, recurring instance, service ordinal, and merchant. Use this cube for appointment booking counts, status analysis, service duration, staff, service, and location reporting.

cubes.name: BankingTransactions

measures.name: BankingTransactions.count
measures.title: Banking Transactions Count

measures.name: BankingTransactions.unique_transactions

measures.name: BankingTransactions.total_amount
measures.title: Banking Transactions Total Amount
measures.description: Total amount of money in main currency units. For asset accounts, positive = money in, negative = money out.

measures.name: BankingTransactions.total_inflow_amount
measures.title: Banking Transactions Total Inflow Amount
measures.description: Total inflow amount (money coming into the account) in main currency units

measures.name: BankingTransactions.total_outflow_amount
measures.title: Banking Transactions Total Outflow Amount
measures.description: Total outflow amount (money leaving the account) in main currency units. Value will be negative.

measures.name: BankingTransactions.avg_transaction_amount
measures.title: Banking Transactions Avg Transaction Amount
measures.description: Average transaction amount in main currency units

dimensions.name: BankingTransactions.event_timestamp

dimensions.name: BankingTransactions.transaction_type

dimensions.name: BankingTransactions.transaction_method

dimensions.name: BankingTransactions.transaction_source
dimensions.title: Banking Transactions Transaction Source

dimensions.name: BankingTransactions.description

dimensions.name: BankingTransactions.balance_type

cubes.title: Banking Transactions

meta.label: Banking Transactions
meta.tooltip: Square Checking account transactions including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and fees

cubes.description: Banking account transactions specifically covering Square Checking account activity (currently does not cover activity from other accounts such as Savings/etc). Each row represents a transaction event (deposit, withdrawal, transfer, fee, etc.) on a seller's internal Square bank account. Includes transaction amounts, running balances, status, type, and method. Use this cube for banking activity reporting — not for sales or order reporting. Queries should be filtered on location_id (if provided), or otherwise grouped by location_id

cubes.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.unique_cash_drawer_shifts
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Unique Cash Drawer Shifts
measures.description: Number of unique cash drawer shifts represented by the selected event rows.

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.cash_movement_event_count
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Cash Movement Event Count
measures.description: Number of cash movement events, excluding shift lifecycle rows.

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.net_cash_amount
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Net Cash Amount
measures.description: Net cash movement amount in main currency units. Cash tender payments and paid-in events are positive; cancelled cash tender payments, cash tender refunds, and paid-out events are negative.

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.cash_in_amount
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Cash in Amount
measures.description: Total cash-in amount in main currency units from cash tender payment and paid-in events.

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.cash_out_amount
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Cash out Amount
measures.description: Total cash-out amount in main currency units from cancelled cash tender payment, cash tender refund, and paid-out events. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.paid_in_event_amount
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Paid in Event Amount
measures.description: Total paid-in cash movement amount in main currency units. Only rows with event_type PAID_IN contribute to this measure.

measures.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.paid_out_event_amount
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Paid out Event Amount
measures.description: Total paid-out cash movement amount in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount. Only rows with event_type PAID_OUT contribute to this measure.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Merchant Id

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.location_id

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.change_type

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.shift_state

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.event_type
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Event Type
dimensions.description: Type of cash movement event. This is null for shift lifecycle rows.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.shift_description
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events Shift Description

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.event_description

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.event_amount

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.event_currency

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.local_day_of_week

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShiftEvents.local_month

cubes.title: Cash Drawer Shift Events

meta.label: Cash drawer shift events
meta.tooltip: Cash drawer shift lifecycle and cash movement events

cubes.description: Cash drawer shift lifecycle and cash movement events. Each row represents either a shift-level state change, such as shift creation or closure, or a cash movement event, such as a cash tender payment, cash tender refund, paid in, or paid out.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.total_cash_tender_cancelled_payments
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Total Cash Tender Cancelled Payments
measures.description: Total cancelled cash tender payment amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.total_cash_tender_refunds
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Total Cash Tender Refunds
measures.description: Total cash tender refund amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.total_paid_in
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Total Paid in
measures.description: Total paid-in amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.total_paid_out
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Total Paid out
measures.description: Total paid-out amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.net_cash_movement
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Net Cash Movement
measures.description: Net cash movement amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Cash tender payments and paid-in events are positive; cancelled cash tender payments, cash tender refunds, and paid-out events are negative.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.expected_cash
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Expected Cash
measures.description: Expected cash amount in main currency units, calculated as starting cash plus cash tender payments and paid in, minus cancelled cash tender payments, cash tender refunds, and paid out.

measures.name: CashDrawerShifts.cash_over_short
measures.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Cash over Short
measures.description: Cash over/short amount in main currency units, calculated as closed cash minus expected cash for shifts with a closed cash amount.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Merchant Id
dimensions.description: The merchant ID associated with the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.location_id
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Location Id
dimensions.description: The location ID where the cash drawer shift occurred.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.team_member_name
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Team Member Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member most directly associated with the shift, preferring the closing employee, then ending employee, then opening employee.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.opening_employee_name
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Opening Employee Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member who opened the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.ending_employee_name
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Ending Employee Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member who ended the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.closing_employee_name
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Closing Employee Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member who closed the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.shift_state
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Shift State
dimensions.description: Latest state of the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.shift_description
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Shift Description
dimensions.description: Seller-entered description for the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.device_id
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Device Id
dimensions.description: Identifier of the device associated with the cash drawer shift. In the source data, this usually corresponds to the base device identifier rather than Devices.device_id.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.device_name
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Device Name
dimensions.description: Display name of the device associated with the cash drawer shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.currency
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Currency
dimensions.description: ISO 4217 currency code for cash amounts on the shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.opened_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Opened at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the cash drawer shift was opened.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_date
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_month
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_day
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_hour
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_minute
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.local_year
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.ended_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Ended at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the cash drawer shift was ended.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.closed_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Closed at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the cash drawer shift was closed.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.created_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Created at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the cash drawer shift was created in the source system.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.updated_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Updated at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the cash drawer shift was last updated in the source system.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.current_balance_updated_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Current Balance Updated at
dimensions.description: Timestamp when the current cash drawer balance was last updated.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.first_cash_movement_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts First Cash Movement at
dimensions.description: Timestamp of the first cash movement event recorded for this shift.

dimensions.name: CashDrawerShifts.last_cash_movement_at
dimensions.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Last Cash Movement at
dimensions.description: Timestamp of the latest cash movement event recorded for this shift.

segments.name: CashDrawerShifts.open_shifts
segments.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Open Shifts
segments.description: Only cash drawer shifts that are currently open.

segments.name: CashDrawerShifts.ended_shifts
segments.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Ended Shifts
segments.description: Only cash drawer shifts that were ended but not closed.

segments.name: CashDrawerShifts.closed_shifts
segments.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Closed Shifts
segments.description: Only cash drawer shifts that were closed.

segments.name: CashDrawerShifts.shifts_with_paid_in
segments.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Shifts with Paid in
segments.description: Only cash drawer shifts with at least one paid-in event.

segments.name: CashDrawerShifts.shifts_with_paid_out
segments.title: Cash Drawer Shifts Shifts with Paid out
segments.description: Only cash drawer shifts with at least one paid-out event.

cubes.title: Cash Drawer Shifts

meta.label: Cash drawer shifts
meta.tooltip: Cash drawer shift closeout totals including paid in and paid out

cubes.description: Cash drawer shifts with one row per shift and pre-aggregated cash movement totals. Use this cube for cash drawer reports such as paid in, paid out, expected cash, closed cash, and cash over/short by location, team member, and shift date.

examples.title: All Catalog Items
examples.description: What are all the items in my catalog?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  CatalogItem.name AS name
FROM CatalogItem
examples.notes: Returns all unique item names from the merchant's catalog. Use in conjunction with 'items-with-sales-in-period' from ItemTransactions cube to find items that haven't sold - compare the two result sets to identify items in the catalog that don't appear in sales.

cubes.description: Catalog Item objects containing detailed item level data such as name, description, item type, category, etc.. Only currently non-deleted items' most recent versions are included. The CatalogItemVariation cube has details (ex. prices) about variations belonging to the items

dimensions.name: CoItemSales.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Co Item Sales Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CoItemSales.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Co Item Sales Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.title: Co Item Sales Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CoItemSales.local_month

dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CoItemSales.local_day
dimensions.title: Co Item Sales Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CoItemSales.local_hour
dimensions.title: Co Item Sales Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: CoItemSales.co_item_name
dimensions.title: Co Item Sales Co Item Name
dimensions.description: The other item on the order. Returns NO_CO_ITEMS when the main item was the only thing on the order.

meta.label: Items sold with
meta.tooltip: Another item on the same order. A row can also indicate that the item sold by itself, meaning nothing else appeared on the order.
meta.context: The other item on the order. When the value is NO_CO_ITEMS, nothing else appeared on the order alongside the main item.

cubes.title: Co Item Sales

meta.label: Items sold together
meta.tooltip: Which items customers buy on the same order

examples.title: Top items sold with a main item
examples.description: Which items were sold most often with a given item in the last 30 days?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  CoItemSales.co_item_name AS co_item_name,
  MEASURE(CoItemSales.pair_orders_count) AS pair_orders_count
FROM CoItemSales
WHERE CoItemSales.anchor_item_name = 'Burger'
  AND CoItemSales.local_reporting_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
  AND CoItemSales.local_reporting_timestamp < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 day'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(CoItemSales.pair_orders_count) DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Filters by anchor_item_name and groups by co_item_name to rank what was sold alongside the anchor item. Results can include a NO_CO_ITEMS row for orders where the anchor item was the only distinct catalog item sold. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

cubes.description: Shows which catalog items sold on the same order, so sellers can answer questions like 'what sells with X?' Each row pairs one item with another item on the same order. Orders where an item sold by itself are also included, so totals cover every order containing that item. Excludes returns. Rolls up all variations of an item under a single item ID.

cubes.title: Customer Snapshots

meta.label: Customers

examples.title: New Customer Visit Count
examples.description: How many new customer visits did I have this month?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MEASURE(CustomerSnapshots.count) AS new_customer_visits
FROM CustomerSnapshots
CROSS JOIN Orders
WHERE CustomerSnapshots.new_customers = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
examples.notes: Counts customer visits where it was the customer's first purchase with this merchant. The new_customers segment filters to only orders where newPerMerchant is TRUE. Uses Orders.local_reporting_timestamp (via ItemTransactions join) for time filtering, which represents the sales timestamp - consistent across all order types including invoices and pre-orders.

cubes.description: Contains information about the customer at the time of each visit (purchase), like if it was their first purchase or if they were loyalty members. Dimensions from this cube can be combined with other cubes to get more information about the customer; each row represents one customer visit

cubes.title: Device Codes

meta.label: Device Codes
meta.tooltip: Device pairing codes and application details for your Square hardware

cubes.description: Device codes represent named stations or points of sale set up by sellers in Square Dashboard. Each device code has a seller-assigned nickname (e.g., 'Hot Drinks', 'Grill', 'Expo', 'Front Register') and is associated with a specific application type and location. For Kitchen Display System (KDS) reporting, device codes are the identity of kitchen stations — the KitchenPerformanceTickets cube joins to this cube via device_id to resolve the human-readable station name. Use DeviceCodes.device_code_name to see which named station processed a ticket, and DeviceCodes.application_type to see the application (e.g., KDS, RESTAURANT, SQUARE_POS)

cubes.title: Devices

meta.label: Devices
meta.tooltip: Your Square devices including hardware type, status, and software updates

cubes.description: Physical devices (iPads, Square Terminals, Square Registers, etc.) used by a Square merchant. Each row represents a single device with its hardware attributes, software version, status, and diagnostics. For kitchen performance reporting, physical devices connect to kitchen stations through the DeviceCodes cube — use DeviceCodes.device_id to join here, not KitchenPerformanceTickets.device_id (which is a device code ID, not a physical device ID). The relationship chain is: KitchenPerformanceTickets → DeviceCodes → Devices

dimensions.name: Fees.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Fees Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Fees Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: Fees.local_date
dimensions.title: Fees Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Fees Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_month
dimensions.title: Fees Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_day
dimensions.title: Fees Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_hour
dimensions.title: Fees Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_minute
dimensions.title: Fees Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Fees.local_year
dimensions.title: Fees Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

examples.title: Units Received For Item In Period
examples.description: How many units of a specific item were received into stock during a time window?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  SUM(InventoryChange.adjustment_quantity) AS units_received
FROM InventoryChange
CROSS JOIN CatalogItemVariation
CROSS JOIN CatalogItem
WHERE CatalogItem.name = 'Example Item'
  AND InventoryChange.change_type = 'ADJUSTMENT'
  AND InventoryChange.adjustment_to_state = 'IN_STOCK'
  AND InventoryChange.event_timestamp >= '2026-01-01'
  AND InventoryChange.event_timestamp < '2026-02-01'
examples.notes: Received events are inferred from `change_type` plus `adjustment_to_state` state transitions. Keep the CROSS JOIN order aligned to the semantic join path: InventoryChange -> CatalogItemVariation -> CatalogItem.

examples.title: Units Ordered For Item In Period
examples.description: How many units of an item variation were ordered from a vendor during a time window?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  SUM(InventoryChange.adjustment_quantity) AS units_ordered
FROM InventoryChange
CROSS JOIN CatalogItemVariation
WHERE InventoryChange.change_type = 'ADJUSTMENT'
  AND InventoryChange.adjustment_from_state = 'NONE'
  AND InventoryChange.adjustment_to_state = 'ORDERED_FROM_VENDOR'
  AND CatalogItemVariation.name = 'Example Item'
  AND InventoryChange.event_timestamp >= '2026-01-01'
  AND InventoryChange.event_timestamp < '2026-02-01'
examples.notes: Purchase order events are inferred by units ordered from adjustments that move stock from `NONE` to `ORDERED_FROM_VENDOR`.

cubes.description: Inventory change events capturing physical counts and adjustments. Each row represents a unique inventory change event, and only the fields relevant to that event's change_type are populated. Use this cube to analyze inventory change patterns/volumes and the operational events that changed inventory. Archived catalog items and variations remain included by default; to exclude them, explicitly join to CatalogItemVariation and CatalogItem and filter the catalog cube's archive state. Use InventoryCount for current quantities and InventoryHistoricalCount for reconstructed past IN_STOCK quantities

examples.title: Sold Out Item Variations
examples.description: Which catalog item variations (non-archived) are out of stock?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  InventoryCount.catalog_object_id,
  MEASURE(InventoryCount.total_quantity) AS qty
FROM InventoryCount
CROSS JOIN CatalogItemVariation
CROSS JOIN CatalogItem
WHERE InventoryCount.state = 'IN_STOCK'
  AND InventoryCount.catalog_object_type = 'ITEM_VARIATION'
  AND CatalogItem.is_archived = false
GROUP BY 1
HAVING MEASURE(InventoryCount.total_quantity) = 0
examples.notes: Joins to CatalogItemVariation and CatalogItem and filter by `IN_STOCK` state to count item variations with 0 stock that are not archived. Keep the CROSS JOIN order aligned to the semantic path: InventoryCount -> CatalogItemVariation -> CatalogItem. Reordering those joins can prevent the planner from resolving CatalogItem fields correctly.

cubes.description: Current inventory quantities by catalog object, location, and stock state. Each row represents the latest known quantity for one (catalog object, location, state) combination. Use this cube for current state-by-state inventory balances. Archived catalog items and variations remain included by default; to exclude them, explicitly join to CatalogItemVariation and CatalogItem and filter the catalog cube's archive state. Use InventoryHistoricalCount for past `IN_STOCK` quantities and InventoryChange for the events that changed inventory

cubes.name: InventoryHistoricalCount

measures.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.count
measures.title: Inventory Historical Count Count
measures.description: Total number of inventory count records

measures.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.current_quantity
measures.title: Inventory Historical Count Current Quantity
measures.description: Current IN_STOCK quantity as of now, before reversing the filtered change window

measures.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.net_stock_change
measures.title: Inventory Historical Count Net Stock Change
measures.description: Net IN_STOCK change within the as_of_date filter range. Positive means stock increased, negative means stock decreased

measures.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.historical_quantity
measures.title: Inventory Historical Count Historical Quantity
measures.description: Historical IN_STOCK quantity, computed as current IN_STOCK minus changes in the as_of_date range. Filter as_of_date to set the target period. This measure does not reconstruct other inventory states

dimensions.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Inventory Historical Count Merchant Id
dimensions.description: The merchant ID

dimensions.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.catalog_object_id
dimensions.title: Inventory Historical Count Catalog Object Id
dimensions.description: The catalog object (item variation) being tracked

dimensions.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.location_id
dimensions.title: Inventory Historical Count Location Id
dimensions.description: The location where the inventory is tracked

dimensions.name: InventoryHistoricalCount.as_of_date
dimensions.title: Inventory Historical Count As of Date
dimensions.description: Filter this dimension to choose the change window to reverse from the current IN_STOCK count. Setting to 'today' reverses today's changes and gives the start-of-day count. Setting to 'last 7 days' reverses the last week of changes and gives the count 7 days ago. For a specific historical date, use an open-ended lower-bound filter like as_of_date >= '2025-10-31' to reverse all later changes. Do not use this dimension for grouping

cubes.title: Inventory Historical Count

meta.label: Inventory History
meta.tooltip: Historical inventory levels over time for tracking stock changes

examples.title: Historical Quantity For Item On Date
examples.description: What was the historical inventory for a specific item on a given day?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MEASURE(InventoryHistoricalCount.historical_quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM InventoryHistoricalCount
CROSS JOIN CatalogItemVariation
CROSS JOIN CatalogItem
WHERE InventoryHistoricalCount.as_of_date >= '2025-10-31'
  AND (
    CatalogItem.name = 'Example Item'
    OR CatalogItemVariation.name = 'Example Item'
  )
examples.notes: For a target historical date, use an open-ended lower-bound filter on as_of_date so all subsequent changes are reversed. Equality on the target date only filters events that happened on that date and will not reconstruct the desired historical balance. Match on either CatalogItem.name or CatalogItemVariation.name when the merchant may refer to the parent item or the specific variation label. Keep the CROSS JOIN order aligned to the semantic path: InventoryHistoricalCount -> CatalogItemVariation -> CatalogItem to prevent join path errors.

cubes.description: Historical inventory in stock quantities reconstructed by reverse-replaying stock changes from the current `IN_STOCK` count. Each row represents one (catalog object, location) pair. Use `historical_quantity` as the direct answer for past stock levels; it is computed from `current_quantity` and `net_stock_change`. To answer 'what was stock on a specific date,' use an open-ended lower-bound filter such as as_of_date >= '2025-10-31' so the cube reverses all changes from that date forward, rather than filtering only events on the target date. Filter the as_of_date dimension to choose which IN_STOCK-affecting changes to reverse — eg. setting the lower bound of as_of_date to 'today' gives the count at the start of today, and 'last 7 days' gives the count 7 days ago. This cube does not provide historical quantities for other inventory states. Use InventoryCount for current quantities by state and InventoryChange for raw inventory events.

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.count

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.current_quantity
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Current Quantity
measures.description: Current IN_STOCK quantity as of now

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.end_of_period_quantity
measures.title: Inventory Metrics End of Period Quantity
measures.description: IN_STOCK quantity at the end of the selected reporting period, reconstructed by subtracting stock changes after the period end from the current quantity

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.on_order_quantity
measures.title: Inventory Metrics On Order Quantity
measures.description: Current quantity on order from vendor (ORDERED_FROM_VENDOR state) as of now

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.in_transit_quantity
measures.title: Inventory Metrics In Transit Quantity
measures.description: Current quantity in transit to this location (IN_TRANSIT state) as of now

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.net_stock_change
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Net Stock Change
measures.description: Net stock change within the reporting period. Positive means stock increased, negative means decreased

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.units_received
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Units Received
measures.description: Net units added into available stock during the reporting period. This includes adjustments into IN_STOCK, minus units sent to transit or moved out to another location. Internal movements cancel out when aggregating across locations

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.starting_count
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Starting Count
measures.description: Starting IN_STOCK quantity at the beginning of the reporting period, computed as end-of-period IN_STOCK minus net stock changes during the reporting period. Uses the same reverse-replay logic as InventoryHistoricalCount

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.units_sold
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Units Sold
measures.description: Units sold from closed orders during the reporting period. Computed from line item quantities in reportable, closed-check orders

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.sales_velocity
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Sales Velocity
measures.description: Average units sold during the reporting period, normalized to a daily, weekly, or monthly cadence based on reporting window length

meta.label: Sales velocity
meta.tooltip: Average units sold during the reporting period, normalized to a display cadence based on the reporting window.
meta.context: Average units sold during the reporting period. Uses per day for windows under 10 days, per week for windows under 3 months, and per month for longer windows.

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.gross_sales
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Gross Sales
measures.description: Gross sales amount for sold items during the reporting period. Computed from closed, reportable order line items and excludes returns

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales amount for sold items only. Does not include returns.
meta.context: Amount of money collected in item sales during the reporting period. Only includes sales, not returns.

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.net_sales
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales amount for sold items during the reporting period. Computed from closed, reportable order line items after discounts and comps, excluding tax and service charges, and excludes returns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales amount for sold items only. Does not include returns.
meta.context: Amount of item sales after discounts and comps during the reporting period. Excludes tax, service charges, and returns.

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.sku_measure
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Sku Measure
measures.description: Stock Keeping Unit for the item variation. Returns MULTIPLE_SKUS when the aggregation contains more than one SKU

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.gtin_measure
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Gtin Measure
measures.description: GTIN for the item variation. Returns MULTIPLE_GTINS when the aggregation contains more than one GTIN

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.category_measure
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Category Measure
measures.description: Reporting category for the item. Returns MULTIPLE_CATEGORIES when the aggregation contains more than one category

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.last_sold_at_measure
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Last Sold at Measure
measures.description: Most recent date when any item variation in the aggregation was sold during the reporting period

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.last_received_at_measure
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Last Received at Measure
measures.description: Most recent date when any item variation in the aggregation received inventory during the reporting period

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.sell_through_rate
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Sell Through Rate
measures.description: Sell-through rate: Units Sold / (Starting Count + Units Received). Measures what fraction of available inventory was sold. Higher values indicate faster-moving inventory

meta.label: Sell-through rate
meta.tooltip: How much of your stock was sold in the period. Higher means faster-moving inventory.
meta.context: How much of your stock was sold in the period. Calculated as units sold divided by available inventory (starting count plus units received). Higher means faster-moving inventory.

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.avg_inventory
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Avg Inventory
measures.description: Average inventory during the reporting period, computed as (starting count + end-of-period quantity) / 2

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.inventory_turnover
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Inventory Turnover
measures.description: Inventory turnover: Units Sold / Average Inventory. Measures how many times inventory was cycled through. Higher values indicate more efficient inventory management

meta.label: Inventory turnover
meta.tooltip: How many times stock was sold and replaced in the period.
meta.context: How many times your stock was sold and replaced during the period. Calculated as units sold divided by average inventory. Higher means more efficient inventory.

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.days_in_period
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Days in Period
measures.description: Number of days in the reporting period, computed from the actual date span of filtered data. Used internally by days_of_supply

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.days_of_supply
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Days of Supply
measures.description: Estimated days of supply remaining at the end of the reporting period, using the daily sales rate from the selected reporting period. NULL when there are no sales in the period

meta.label: Days of supply
meta.tooltip: Estimated days until stock runs out at the current sales pace.
meta.context: Estimated days until stock runs out at the current sales pace. Calculated from current inventory divided by your average daily sales (units sold divided by days in the period).

measures.name: InventoryMetrics.estimated_sold_out_at
measures.title: Inventory Metrics Estimated Sold out at
measures.description: Estimated date when stock will run out, calculated as the end of the reporting period plus days of supply, capped at 2 years out. NULL when there are no sales in the period

meta.label: Estimated sold out date
meta.tooltip: Estimated date when stock will run out at the current sales pace.
meta.context: Estimated sold out date based on ending on-hand quantity and sales velocity. Calculated as the end of the reporting period plus days of supply.

cubes.title: Inventory Metrics

meta.label: Inventory Metrics
meta.tooltip: Calculated inventory metrics like sell-through rate, turnover, and days of supply

cubes.description: Inventory performance metrics combining stock levels with sales data. Each row represents one item variation at one location for the selected reporting period. Use this cube for KPI analysis such as sell-through rate, inventory turnover, average inventory, and days of supply. For historical period calculations, filter both reporting_period and post_reporting_period; post_reporting_period should use an open-ended lower bound starting immediately after the reporting period. Archived catalog items and variations remain included by default; to exclude them, explicitly join to CatalogItemVariation and CatalogItem and filter the catalog cube's archive state. Use InventoryCount for current quantities by state, InventoryHistoricalCount for reconstructed past IN_STOCK quantities, and InventoryChange for raw inventory events

cubes.description: Default inventory tracking state per catalog object. Each row represents the latest tracking configuration for a given merchant and catalog object, including the default tracking state that applies to all locations unless overridden. Catalog objects not present in this table should be considered NOT_TRACKING. Archived catalog items and variations remain included by default; to exclude them, explicitly join to CatalogItemVariation and CatalogItem and filter the catalog cube's archive state. Use InventoryTrackingOverrides for per-location overrides; if an override exists for a location, it takes precedence over this default

dimensions.name: ItemDiscountsAndComps.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Item Discounts and Comps Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ItemDiscountsAndComps.local_hour

dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: ItemDiscountsAndComps.check_state
dimensions.title: Item Discounts and Comps Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

cubes.title: Item Discounts and Comps

meta.label: Discounts & Comps
meta.tooltip: Discounts and comps applied to line items, with amounts and types

examples.title: Total Discount Loss
examples.description: How much did I lose on discounts this quarter?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MEASURE(ItemDiscountsAndComps.total_discount_and_comp_amount) AS total_discount_and_comp_amount
FROM ItemDiscountsAndComps
WHERE ItemDiscountsAndComps.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND ItemDiscountsAndComps.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND ItemDiscountsAndComps.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '3 months'
examples.notes: Calculates the total amount of discounts and comps given during a specific time period. Uses total_discount_and_comp_amount measure which includes both discounts and comps, with sales as positive values and returns as negative values (netting out the impact). The closed_checks segment ensures only completed orders are included. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

cubes.description: Represents discounts and comps applied to line items. Sold items have positive amounts, returned have negative amounts. This cube can be used to query for overall discount metrics by grouping on discount type and name, or to query for discounted line item amounts by grouping by line item level dimensions instead.

dimensions.name: ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp

dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ItemTransactions.check_state
dimensions.title: Item Transactions Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

cubes.title: Item Transactions

meta.label: Item Sales
meta.tooltip: Item-level sales and returns data including quantities, prices, and categories

examples.title: Items With Sales in Period
examples.description: What items have sold this month?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  ItemTransactions.item_name AS item_name
FROM ItemTransactions
WHERE ItemTransactions.sales = TRUE
  AND ItemTransactions.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
GROUP BY 1
HAVING MEASURE(ItemTransactions.sales_quantity) > 0
examples.notes: Returns unique item names that had at least one sale in the specified period. Uses timeDimensions with local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering. Filters to sales transactions only (excludes returns) and closed checks. Use in conjunction with 'all-catalog-items' from CatalogItem cube to find unsold items - items in the catalog that don't appear here haven't sold in the period.

examples.title: Item Price Change Over Time
examples.description: How has the price of specific items changed from start of 2025 to end of October?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  ItemTransactions.local_month AS local_month,
  ItemTransactions.item_name AS item_name,
  MEASURE(ItemTransactions.avg_item_gross_sales) AS avg_item_gross_sales,
  MEASURE(ItemTransactions.sales_quantity) AS sales_quantity
FROM ItemTransactions
WHERE ItemTransactions.item_name IN ('FRIES', 'BURGER')
  AND ItemTransactions.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp < '2025-11-01'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY ItemTransactions.local_month ASC
examples.notes: Analyzes how the average price (avg_item_gross_sales) and quantity sold for one or more items changes month-by-month. The item_name filter accepts either a single item name (e.g., values: ['FRIES']) or multiple item names (e.g., values: ['FRIES', 'BURGER', 'SODA']) to compare pricing trends across items. When multiple items are provided, the query includes item_name as a dimension to show separate rows for each item per month. Uses local_reporting_timestamp in timeDimensions for timezone-aware filtering and local_month dimension for monthly grouping. Orders results chronologically. The closed_checks segment ensures only completed orders are included. Combines pricing and volume metrics to understand both price trends and demand patterns.

examples.title: Sales by Category in Date Range
examples.description: What are my sales and quantities by category between two dates?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  ItemTransactions.category_name AS category_name,
  MEASURE(ItemTransactions.sales_gross_amount) AS sales_gross_amount,
  MEASURE(ItemTransactions.sales_quantity) AS sales_quantity
FROM ItemTransactions
WHERE ItemTransactions.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND ItemTransactions.local_reporting_timestamp < '2025-11-01'
GROUP BY 1
examples.notes: Shows gross sales and quantity sold broken down by category for a specific date range. Uses timeDimensions with local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware date filtering. The closed_checks segment ensures only completed transactions are counted. Items without a category will show as 'Uncategorized' (handled by the category_name dimension's COALESCE).

cubes.description: Financial transaction data for line items, including sales and returns. Sold items have positive amounts, returned items have negative amounts. This cube is used to query for item-level transaction data, including quantity, money amounts, categories, etc.

cubes.name: KitchenPerformanceItems

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.count
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Count
measures.description: Number of line items (rows). Each row is one line item on one station's kitchen ticket. A line item with quantity 3 is one row (count=1, quantity_sold=3) — use quantity_sold to sum units. The same line item routed through multiple stations (prep + expo) produces multiple rows. Use this for discrete line-item questions (e.g., recalls, % recalled). Exposed as 'items_count' on the KDS view.

meta.label: Line items
meta.tooltip: Number of line items (rows) on kitchen tickets.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.quantity_sold
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Quantity Sold
measures.description: Total quantity of items

meta.label: Total quantity
meta.tooltip: Total quantity of items prepared.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.avg_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Avg Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Average (mean) time in seconds from when the ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when this specific item was marked complete. Use this to identify which items take longest to prepare. Group by item_name and station_type to see how item prep times vary across station roles. WARNING: heavily skewed by outlier items that were left on open tickets for hours. For "typical" or representative item completion time, prefer median_item_time_seconds.

meta.label: Avg item time
meta.tooltip: Average seconds from KDS display to item completion. Prefer median if outliers are a concern.
meta.context: Measures how long an individual item takes from the moment its ticket appears on a station's screen until the item is marked done. Unlike the ticket-level avg_ticket_time_seconds (in KitchenPerformanceTickets), this measures each item independently — useful for identifying slow-cooking items or items that bottleneck a station. For seller-facing "typical time" questions, prefer median_item_time_seconds because the mean can be heavily skewed by items that were left on tickets that stayed open for hours.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.min_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Min Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Minimum time in seconds from ticket display to item completion

meta.label: Min prep time
meta.tooltip: Minimum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.max_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Max Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Maximum time in seconds from ticket display to item completion

meta.label: Max prep time
meta.tooltip: Maximum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.median_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Median Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Median time in seconds from when the ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when this specific item was marked complete. More robust than average for identifying typical item prep times because it is not skewed by outlier items

meta.label: Median item time
meta.tooltip: Median seconds from ticket display on the KDS screen to this item being marked complete.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.recalled_quantity
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Recalled Quantity
measures.description: Total summed quantity of recalled items. Because quantity is a DOUBLE, fractional units (e.g. bananas sold by weight) inflate this measure beyond a simple count of recall events. Prefer recall_count when a seller asks "how many items were recalled?" and prefer recall_rate when they ask for a percentage. Use recalled_quantity only when the seller explicitly cares about summed units (e.g., "how many recalled units did we ring up?").

meta.label: Recalled items
meta.tooltip: Total summed quantity of items that were recalled.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.recall_count
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Recall Count
measures.description: Number of line items that were recalled. Each row counts as 1 regardless of quantity, so this is the right measure for "how many items were recalled?", "count of recalls", or for the numerator of a recall rate. Differs from recalled_quantity, which sums the quantity column and therefore inflates for items priced by weight or with quantity > 1.

meta.label: Recall count
meta.tooltip: Number of line items that were recalled.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.recall_rate
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Recall Rate
measures.description: Percentage of line items that were recalled, returned as a number between 0 and 100. Use this for "what is the recall rate?", "% recalled", or "% of items remade" questions. Computed as 100 * recall_count / line_item_count, so both numerator and denominator are row counts (not quantity sums). Group by station_type, device_code_name, or item_name to compare recall rates across stations or items.

meta.label: Recall rate
meta.tooltip: Percentage of line items that were recalled.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.avg_time_vs_due_seconds
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Avg Time Vs Due Seconds
measures.description: Average difference in seconds between item completion and time due (positive = late, negative = early, 0 if no time due)

meta.label: Avg time vs target
meta.tooltip: Average difference in seconds between completion and target time.
meta.context: Positive values indicate late completion, negative values indicate early completion. Zero if no target time was set.

measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Quantity with Time Due

meta.label: Items with target time
meta.tooltip: Quantity of items with a target completion time.

measures.title: Kitchen Performance Items Quantity Completed Late

meta.label: Late items
meta.tooltip: Quantity of items completed after their target time.

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.percent_late

meta.label: Percent late

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_date
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_month
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_day
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_hour
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_minute
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceItems.local_year
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Items Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

cubes.title: Kitchen Performance Items

meta.label: Kitchen Items
meta.tooltip: Individual item performance in the kitchen display system (KDS)

examples.title: Most Common Items with Median Prep Time
examples.description: What are the most common items on KDS tickets in 2025, and which takes longest to prepare?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  KDS.item_name,
  MEASURE(KDS.quantity_sold) AS quantity_sold,
  MEASURE(KDS.median_item_time_seconds) AS median_prep_secs
FROM KDS
WHERE KDS.local_date >= '2025-01-01'
  AND KDS.local_date < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1
HAVING MEASURE(KDS.quantity_sold) >= 10
ORDER BY median_prep_secs DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Uses KDS.quantity_sold (not ItemSales.items_sold_count) for kitchen item volume, and median_item_time_seconds for robust prep time estimation. The HAVING clause filters to items with at least 10 prepared units to focus on common items. For item sales revenue, use the ItemSales view instead.

examples.title: Recall Count and Recall Rate by Station
examples.description: Which kitchen stations had the highest recall rate this month, and how many line items were recalled?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  KDS.station_type,
  KDS.device_code_name,
  MEASURE(KDS.items_count) AS items,
  MEASURE(KDS.recall_count) AS recalls,
  MEASURE(KDS.recall_rate) AS recall_rate_pct
FROM KDS
WHERE KDS.local_year = 2025
  AND KDS.local_month = 12
GROUP BY 1, 2
HAVING MEASURE(KDS.items_count) >= 25
ORDER BY recall_rate_pct DESC
examples.notes: Uses recall_count (count of recalled rows) and recall_rate (percentage), not recalled_quantity (which sums the quantity column and inflates for items priced by weight). The HAVING clause filters out tiny denominators so the rate is meaningful. Drop the station_type / device_code_name group-by to get an overall merchant-wide recall rate.

cubes.description: Kitchen Display System (KDS) line-item-level performance data. Each row represents a single line item from a completed kitchen ticket, with item-specific timing information. Use this cube to analyze individual item preparation times, identify slow-cooking items, and optimize kitchen workflows at the item level. Each item is scoped to a specific kitchen station — a named KDS screen in the kitchen (e.g., 'Hot Drinks', 'Grill'). The same item can appear on multiple stations if the chit flows through Prep and then Expo. To see station names, join to KitchenPerformanceTickets and then to DeviceCodes. To filter by station role, use the station_type dimension

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.count

measures.description: Count of kitchen tickets (chits per station). Each ticket represents one station's view of a kitchen order. The same order may produce multiple tickets if it is routed to multiple stations (e.g., Prep then Expo). Exposed as 'ticket_count' on the KDS view and 'kds_ticket_count' on the SpeedOfService view

measures.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.avg_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Avg Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Average (mean) time in seconds from when a ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when the last item on that ticket was marked complete. WARNING: heavily skewed by outlier tickets that were left open for hours (e.g., never marked done at end of shift). For 'typical' or representative completion time, prefer median_ticket_time_seconds. Group by station_type to compare Prep vs. Expo performance, or join to DeviceCodes and group by DeviceCodes.device_code_name to compare individual stations

meta.label: Avg ticket time
meta.tooltip: Average seconds from ticket display on the KDS screen to completion of the last item. Skewed by outliers — prefer median for typical completion time.
meta.context: Measures how long a station takes to complete all items on a ticket. The clock starts when the ticket first appears on the station's screen (display_on_kds_at) and stops when the last line item is marked done (actual_completed_at). The mean is sensitive to outlier tickets — tickets occasionally remain open for hours and inflate the mean by orders of magnitude. For seller-facing 'typical time' questions, prefer median_ticket_time_seconds, which is robust to outliers without imposing an arbitrary cutoff. To identify slow stations, group this measure by DeviceCodes.device_code_name or station_type.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.station_type
