dimensions.description: The role of the kitchen station that processed this ticket. `kds` stations are prep/make-line stations where food and drinks are prepared (e.g., a 'Grill' station or 'Hot Drinks' station). `kds_expo` stations are expeditor stations where completed items are reviewed and assembled before being sent to the customer. To see the specific station name (e.g., 'Hot Drinks'), join to DeviceCodes and use DeviceCodes.device_code_name

meta.label: Station type
meta.tooltip: The role of the kitchen station: Prep (where items are made) or Expo (where orders are assembled and reviewed before serving).
meta.context: Stations are the individual KDS screens in a kitchen, each assigned a role. `kds`: A preparation/make-line station where kitchen staff make specific items (e.g., a grill station, a drinks station). `kds_expo`: An expeditor station that reviews and assembles completed items from prep stations before they go to the customer. A single kitchen can have many `kds` (prep) stations but typically one or two `kds_expo` stations. To compare performance between individual stations (not just station types), join to the DeviceCodes cube and group by DeviceCodes.device_code_name.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.device_id
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Device Id
dimensions.description: The device code ID of the KDS station that processed this ticket. Despite the name, this is a device code identifier (not a physical device ID). Each device code represents a named station in the kitchen (e.g., 'Hot Drinks', 'Grill'). Join to the DeviceCodes cube to get the human-readable station name (DeviceCodes.device_code_name) or to the Devices cube (via DeviceCodes) for physical device details

meta.label: Station device code ID
meta.tooltip: The device code ID identifying which kitchen station processed this ticket. Join to DeviceCodes for the station name.
meta.context: This field contains the device code ID, which is the unique identifier for a named KDS station. Sellers create device codes in Square Dashboard under Settings > Device Management > Device Codes, where they assign a name (like 'Hot Drinks' or 'Grill') and a role (Prep or Expo). To get the station name, join this cube to DeviceCodes using device_id = DeviceCodes.device_code_id, then use DeviceCodes.device_code_name.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_date
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_month
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_day
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_hour
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_minute
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KitchenPerformanceTickets.local_year
dimensions.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

cubes.title: Kitchen Performance Tickets

meta.label: Kitchen Tickets
meta.tooltip: Ticket-level kitchen performance including prep times and on-time rates

examples.title: Top Station by Kitchen Tickets Completed
examples.description: Which station completed the most kitchen tickets?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Location.location_name AS location_name,
  DeviceCodes.device_code_name AS device_code_name,
  MEASURE(KitchenPerformanceTickets.count) AS count
FROM KitchenPerformanceTickets
CROSS JOIN Location
CROSS JOIN DeviceCodes
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY MEASURE(KitchenPerformanceTickets.count) DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Shows which KDS stations have completed the most tickets across all time, grouped by location and station name. Requires joins to both Location (for location_name) and DeviceCodes (for device_code_name).

examples.title: Percentage of Tickets Completed Within a Time Threshold
examples.description: What percentage of tickets were completed within 10 minutes in February 2026?
examples.sql_query:
WITH tickets AS (
  SELECT
    KDS.ticket_key,
    MEASURE(KDS.avg_ticket_time_seconds) AS time_seconds
  FROM KDS
  WHERE KDS.local_date >= '2026-02-01'
    AND KDS.local_date < '2026-03-01'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total_tickets,
  SUM(CASE WHEN time_seconds <= 600 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS within_threshold,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN time_seconds <= 600 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct
FROM tickets
examples.notes: Use a CTE to get per-ticket completion times by grouping by ticket_key, then filter or bucket in the outer query. This pattern is required for any question that needs per-ticket time thresholds (e.g., '% within X minutes', 'how many tickets over Y hours'). The inner query uses avg_ticket_time_seconds which, when grouped by ticket_key, returns each ticket's individual completion time in seconds.

examples.title: Weekly Station Efficiency Scorecard
examples.description: How does each station perform week over week — what percentage of tickets are completed under 5 minutes, and how many exceed 30 minutes?
examples.sql_query:
WITH weekly_station AS (
  SELECT
    KDS.location_name,
    KDS.device_code_name,
    KDS.station_type,
    DATE_TRUNC('week', KDS.local_date) AS week_start,
    KDS.ticket_key,
    MEASURE(KDS.avg_ticket_time_seconds) AS time_seconds
  FROM KDS
  WHERE KDS.local_date >= '2026-01-01'
    AND KDS.local_date < '2026-04-01'
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
)
SELECT
  location_name,
  device_code_name,
  station_type,
  week_start,
  COUNT(*) AS total_tickets,
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_APPROX(time_seconds, 0.5), 0) AS median_secs,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN time_seconds <= 300 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct_under_5min,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN time_seconds > 1800 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    / COUNT(*), 1) AS pct_over_30min
FROM weekly_station
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20
ORDER BY week_start, pct_under_5min ASC
examples.notes: Demonstrates several Cube SQL patterns in one query: (1) CTE groups by ticket_key to get per-ticket times, then re-aggregates by station and week. (2) MEASURE() wraps avg_ticket_time_seconds — required in Cube SQL for all measure references. (3) location_name and device_code_name are accessed directly on the KDS view without CROSS JOIN (joins are built into the view). (4) local_date is a timezone-aware dimension from the Location cube. (5) DATE_TRUNC on local_date creates weekly buckets. (6) HAVING filters to stations with meaningful volume.

cubes.description: Kitchen Display System (KDS) ticket-level performance data. Each row represents a completed kitchen ticket (chit) with timing information for measuring kitchen throughput and efficiency. Use this cube to analyze ticket completion times, station performance, and order flow through the kitchen. A 'station' is a specific work area in the kitchen paired with a KDS screen (e.g., 'Hot Drinks', 'Grill', 'Expo'). Stations are set up in Square Dashboard under Device Management using device codes. The same order can appear on multiple stations — for example, first on a Prep station and then on an Expo station — so each row is uniquely identified by the combination of chit and station (ticket_key). To get the station name, join to the DeviceCodes cube via device_id. To filter by station role (Prep vs. Expo), use the station_type dimension

meta.label: Marketing Campaigns
meta.tooltip: Marketing campaign details including send status, channels, and scheduling

examples.title: Sent Email Campaigns with Delivery Counts
examples.description: Which email campaigns were sent between April 1st 2026 and April 6th, 2026 and what were their delivery counts?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MarketingPromotions.promotion_id,
  MarketingPromotions.subject,
  MarketingPromotions.sent_at,
  MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.count) AS delivery_count
FROM MarketingPromotions
CROSS JOIN MarketingDeliveries
WHERE MarketingPromotions.channel = 'EMAIL'
  AND MarketingPromotions.sent_at >= '2026-04-01'
  AND MarketingPromotions.sent_at < '2026-04-07'
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
ORDER BY 3 ASC
examples.notes: Use `sent_at` to filter for campaigns that were sent.

examples.title: Email Campaign Open and Click Rate
examples.description: What was the open and click rate for my April 1 campaign, '<Campaign Name>'?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MarketingPromotions.promotion_id,
  MarketingPromotions.subject,
  MarketingPromotions.sent_at,
  MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.processed_count) AS deliveries,
  MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.opened_count) AS opened_deliveries,
  MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.clicked_count) AS clicked_deliveries,
  CASE
    WHEN MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.processed_count) > 0
      THEN ROUND(
        100.0 * MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.opened_count)
        / MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.processed_count),
        1
      )
    ELSE 0
  END AS open_rate,
  CASE
    WHEN MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.processed_count) > 0
      THEN ROUND(
        100.0 * MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.clicked_count)
        / MEASURE(MarketingDeliveries.processed_count),
        1
      )
    ELSE 0
  END AS click_rate
FROM MarketingPromotions
CROSS JOIN MarketingDeliveries
WHERE MarketingPromotions.subject = '<Campaign Name>'
  AND MarketingPromotions.channel = 'EMAIL'
  AND MarketingPromotions.sent_at >= '2026-04-01'
  AND MarketingPromotions.sent_at < '2026-04-02'
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
examples.notes: Use `subject` to identify the campaign name. `processed_count` is used as the denominator so the ratio computes open and click rates across successful deliveries.

dimensions.name: ModifiersTransacted.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Modifiers Transacted Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ModifiersTransacted.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Modifiers Transacted Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: ModifiersTransacted.local_month
dimensions.title: Modifiers Transacted Local Month

dimensions.name: ModifiersTransacted.local_day

dimensions.name: ModifiersTransacted.local_hour
dimensions.title: Modifiers Transacted Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: ModifiersTransacted.check_state
dimensions.title: Modifiers Transacted Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

cubes.title: Modifiers Transacted

meta.label: Modifier Sales
meta.tooltip: Modifier-level sales and returns data with quantities and amounts

examples.title: Best-Selling Modifiers
examples.description: Which modifiers are the best-selling (most sold) from start of 2025 to end of October?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  CatalogModifierList.name AS name,
  ModifiersTransacted.modifier_name AS modifier_name,
  MEASURE(ModifiersTransacted.quantity_sold) AS quantity_sold,
  MEASURE(ModifiersTransacted.gross_sales) AS gross_sales
FROM ModifiersTransacted
CROSS JOIN CatalogModifierList
WHERE ModifiersTransacted.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND ModifiersTransacted.sales_only = TRUE
  AND ModifiersTransacted.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND ModifiersTransacted.local_reporting_timestamp < '2025-11-01'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY MEASURE(ModifiersTransacted.gross_sales) DESC, CatalogModifierList.name ASC, ModifiersTransacted.modifier_name ASC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Shows the best-selling modifiers by gross sales during the specified period. Uses closed_checks and sales_only segments to filter to only completed sales transactions. Orders by gross_sales descending to show most sold modifiers first, then alphabetically by modifier list name and modifier name. Includes quantity_sold to show volume impact of each modifier. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

examples.title: Top Selling Modifier Sets
examples.description: Which modifier sets generated the most revenue?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  CatalogModifierList.name AS name,
  MEASURE(ModifiersTransacted.gross_sales) AS gross_sales,
  MEASURE(ModifiersTransacted.quantity_sold) AS quantity_sold
FROM ModifiersTransacted
CROSS JOIN CatalogModifierList
WHERE ModifiersTransacted.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND ModifiersTransacted.sales_only = TRUE
  AND ModifiersTransacted.local_reporting_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
  AND ModifiersTransacted.local_reporting_timestamp < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 day'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(ModifiersTransacted.gross_sales) DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Shows the top selling modifier sets (lists) by gross sales during the specified period. Groups by modifier list name to show aggregate performance at the set level rather than individual modifiers. Uses closed_checks and sales_only segments to filter to only completed sales transactions. Orders by gross_sales descending to show highest revenue modifier sets first. Includes quantity_sold to show volume alongside revenue. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

dimensions.description: Unique ID for the order

meta.label: Order ID
meta.tooltip: Unique ID associated with the order.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.payment_id
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Payment Id
dimensions.description: Unique ID for the payment

meta.label: Payment ID
meta.tooltip: Unique ID associated with the payment.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description:
For payments processed by Square, this field will initially be `null`, and will get set
depending on the type of payment method. It will be set based on the following conditions:
- Card and Payments Modeled as Card (Afterpay, PayPay, etc): Set to the record's `authIntent.createdAt`
- Square Gift Cards: Set to the record's `captureIntent.createdAt`
- Bank Transfers: Set to the record's `bankTransaction.completedAt`
- Async Transaction: Set to the record's `asyncTransaction.completedAt`

On failed payments, this field will remain unset permanently

For payments recorded but not processed by Square (which can never fail, and have no
processing lifecycle), this field will always be set, typically to the `createdAt` timestamp

meta.label: Payment timestamp
meta.tooltip: Time when the payment was recorded.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_date
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_month
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_day
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_hour
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_minute
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: OfflinePayments.local_year
dimensions.title: Offline Payments Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

cubes.title: Offline Payments
cubes.description: Offline payments data. Includes only payments (not refunds) where the order's payment group has completionProcessingMode set to OFFLINE. Includes FAILED payment status in addition to APPROVED, COMPLETED, and PENDING.

cubes.name: Orders

measures.name: Orders.count
measures.title: Orders Count
measures.description: Total number of orders

meta.label: Number of orders
meta.tooltip: Total number of orders sold.
meta.context: The total number of orders sold.

measures.name: Orders.top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Orders Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries - This is the top-line product sales without counting refunds, discounts, comps, refunds, tips, tax, or fees, or sales of gift cards. This is the default measure when 'sales' is referenced ambiguously without specifying net, total, or other qualifiers.

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of money collected in sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, returns, tips, taxes, fees, and gift card sales.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales
measures.title: Orders Net Sales
measures.description: Top line sales after discounts and itemized returns. Equivalent to 'topLineProductSales' - discountsAndComps - itemizedReturns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.
meta.context: Net sales are equal to your gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.

measures.name: Orders.covered_net_sales
measures.title: Orders Covered Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales from orders with one or more covers

meta.label: Covered net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers.
meta.context: Covered net sales only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover.

measures.name: Orders.cover_count
measures.title: Orders Cover Count
measures.description: Number of covers served by the orders

meta.label: Covers
meta.tooltip: Number of covers served.
meta.context: Covers are based on the number of seats on the order's restaurant seating extension.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_per_cover
measures.title: Orders Net Sales Per Cover
measures.description: Average net sales per cover, excluding orders without covers from the numerator

meta.label: Net sales per cover
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover is calculated as net sales from orders with covers divided by total covers.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_with_tax
measures.title: Orders Net Sales with Tax
measures.description: Net sales with sales tax added. Also called Gross Sales in AU and similar countries

meta.label: Gross Sales (AU and similar countries)
meta.tooltip: Amount of net sales including sales tax.
meta.context: This is your net sales amount, but with sales tax included.

measures.name: Orders.itemized_returns
measures.title: Orders Itemized Returns
measures.description: Itemized returns. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales. Also known as GrossSalesReturns

meta.label: Returns
meta.tooltip: Amount of money returned for item sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money returned to customers on sales of items. This excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.

measures.name: Orders.comps_amount
measures.title: Orders Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

measures.name: Orders.discounts_amount
measures.title: Orders Discounts Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary discount deduction, including returns. Discounts applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.label: Discounts
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: Orders.sales_tax_amount
measures.title: Orders Sales Tax Amount
measures.description: Sales tax amounts applied at the time of sale. Configured by the seller

meta.label: Sales tax
meta.tooltip: Total amount of sales tax applied to an order.
meta.context: The total amount of sales tax applied to an order. This is configured in Square based on your tax jurisdictions.

measures.name: Orders.tips_amount
measures.title: Orders Tips Amount
measures.description: Total of all tips excluding cash tips. Excludes auto gratuity which is a service charge and cash tips since cash tips are not recorded in the Square App

meta.label: Tips (non-cash)
meta.tooltip: Total amount of tips collected. Does not include cash tips or autogratuity.
meta.context: The total amount of tips you collected through Square. This does not include cash tips or autogratuity, which is a service charge.

measures.name: Orders.gift_card_sales_amount
measures.title: Orders Gift Card Sales Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the sale of Square Gift Cards. Note this metric refers to sales of gift cards, not items purchased using gift cards as a currency. Includes Discounts & Returns. Excludes taxes on gift card sales and surcharges on gift card sales

meta.label: Gift card sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected from the sale of Square gift cards. Includes discounts and returns. Excludes taxes and surcharges.
meta.context: The total amount you collected from the sale of Square gift cards. This does not refer to sales made to customers who pay with a gift card. The amount includes discounts and returns but excludes taxes and surcharges on gift card sales.

measures.name: Orders.refunds_by_amount_amount
measures.title: Orders Refunds by Amount Amount
measures.description: A non-itemized refund that is purely a money amount given back to the customer. Negative value means less money collected for the sale

meta.label: Refunds
meta.tooltip: Amount of money refunded to a customer for an order.
meta.context: The amount of money refunded to a customer. If this number is negative, it means that you refunded the customer more money than you collected from them for the order.

measures.name: Orders.swedish_cash_rounding_amount
measures.title: Orders Swedish Cash Rounding Amount
measures.description: Adjustments applied according to the Swedish Rounding System. Applies in countries where low denomination coins have been discontinued. Rounding is applied on the whole order (not each individual item) after discounts, tax, and tip are applied

meta.label: Rounding adjustment
meta.tooltip: Rounding adjustments made to an order after discounts, taxes, and tips are applied.
meta.context: The rounding adjustment made to an entire order after discounts, taxes, and tips are applied. This exists in countries where small-denomination coins have been discontinued.

measures.name: Orders.total_collected_amount
measures.title: Orders Total Collected Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the customer. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, tax, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding. aka Total Sales

meta.label: Total collected
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected in sales. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, taxes, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding.
meta.context: The total amount you collected in sales. This includes the amount you collected in modifiers, tips, taxes, and gift card sales. It accounts for discounts provided, returns, refunds, and cash rounding as well.

measures.name: Orders.item_sales_amount
measures.title: Orders Item Sales Amount
measures.description: Total item sales including modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales

meta.label: Total item sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount of collected from items sold. Includes modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money collected from items you sold. Modifiers are counted as items. But returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales are excluded.

measures.name: Orders.deposits_redeemed_amount
measures.title: Orders Deposits Redeemed Amount
measures.description: Amount of deposits redeemed on this order. Deposits are prepayments that customers make that get applied to orders later

meta.label: Deposits redeemed
meta.tooltip: Total amount of deposits redeemed on orders.
meta.context: The total amount of deposits (prepayments) that were applied to orders. This represents money that customers paid in advance and later used toward their purchase.

measures.name: Orders.total_sales_amount
measures.title: Orders Total Sales Amount
measures.description: Total sales amount for the order

meta.label: Total sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales minus discounts/comps/returns, plus taxes/tips/gift cards, minus refunds.
meta.context: Calculated as gross sales (item sales + surcharges) minus discounts, comps, and returns, plus taxes, tips, and gift card sales, then minus refunds. Provides a complete view of all sales activity.

measures.name: Orders.avg_total_sales
measures.title: Orders Avg Total Sales
measures.description: Average total sales per order

meta.label: Average total sales
meta.tooltip: Average total sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average total sales amount across all of your orders.

measures.name: Orders.most_recent_sale
measures.title: Orders Most Recent Sale
measures.description: The timestamp of the most recent sale in the selected time period or filter set

meta.label: Most recent sale
meta.tooltip: Timestamp of the most recent sale.
meta.context: The date and time of the most recent sale within the selected time period or filter criteria.

measures.name: Orders.avg_net_sales
measures.title: Orders Avg Net Sales
measures.description: Average net sales per order

meta.label: Average net sales
meta.tooltip: Average net sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of net sales (gross sales minus...) you made across all of your orders.

measures.name: Orders.avg_total_collected
measures.title: Orders Avg Total Collected
measures.description: Average total collected amount per order

meta.label: Average total collected
meta.tooltip: Average amount collected in total from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of money you collected across all of your orders.

measures.name: Orders.avg_top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Orders Avg Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Average gross sales per order

meta.label: Average gross sales
meta.tooltip: Average amount of gross sales per order.
meta.context: The average amount of gross sales (top-line product sales) across all of your orders.

measures.name: Orders.avg_order_entry_time_ms
measures.title: Orders Avg Order Entry Time Ms
measures.description: Average time to fully enter an order, from entry start to first item send, in milliseconds

meta.label: Avg order entry time
meta.tooltip: Average time between when the first item is added to an order and when the first item is sent to the kitchen.

measures.name: Orders.avg_service_time_ms
measures.title: Orders Avg Service Time Ms
measures.description: Average total time of service, from order entry to payment, in milliseconds

meta.label: Avg service time
meta.tooltip: Average time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: Orders.max_order_entry_time_ms
measures.title: Orders Max Order Entry Time Ms
measures.description: Maximum order entry time in milliseconds

meta.label: Max order entry time
meta.tooltip: Longest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the first item is sent to the kitchen.

measures.name: Orders.max_service_time_ms
measures.title: Orders Max Service Time Ms
measures.description: Maximum service time in milliseconds

meta.label: Max service time
meta.tooltip: Longest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: Orders.min_order_entry_time_ms
measures.title: Orders Min Order Entry Time Ms
measures.description: Minimum order entry time in milliseconds

meta.label: Min order entry time
meta.tooltip: Shortest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the first item is sent to the kitchen.

measures.name: Orders.min_service_time_ms
measures.title: Orders Min Service Time Ms
measures.description: Minimum service time in milliseconds

meta.label: Min service time
meta.tooltip: Shortest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: Orders.auto_gratuity_amount
measures.title: Orders Auto Gratuity Amount
measures.description: Total automatic gratuity charged on orders. Autogratuity is a service charge, not a tip. Used for UK-specific metrics.

meta.label: Service charges payable to employees
meta.tooltip: Total service charges payable to employees charged on orders. Excludes returns and service charge tax.

measures.name: Orders.auto_gratuity_tax_amount
measures.title: Orders Auto Gratuity Tax Amount
measures.description: Tax applied to automatic gratuity charges

meta.label: Service charge tax (Payable to Employees)
meta.tooltip: Tax collected on service charges payable to employees.

measures.name: Orders.returned_auto_gratuity_amount
measures.title: Orders Returned Auto Gratuity Amount
measures.description: Automatic gratuity amounts returned to customers

meta.label: Returned service charges payable to employees
meta.tooltip: Service charges payable to employees refunded on returns.

measures.name: Orders.top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Top Line Product Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Top Line Product Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Top line product sales excluding automatic gratuity. UK compliance metric. Equivalent to top_line_product_sales minus auto_gratuity_amount

meta.label: Top line product sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Top line product sales excluding service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: Orders.service_charges_only_sum
measures.title: Orders Service Charges Only Sum
measures.description: Service charges excluding card surcharges and automatic gratuity. UK sales summary metric.

meta.label: Other service charges
meta.tooltip: Service charges passed on to customers, excluding service charges payable to employees and card surcharges.

measures.name: Orders.returns_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Returns Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Itemized returns excluding returned automatic gratuity. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Returns (UK)
meta.tooltip: Returns excluding service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: Orders.discount_and_comps_sum
measures.title: Orders Discount and Comps Sum
measures.description: Combined signed discounts and comps as shown in Sales Summary. Equivalent to discounts_amount plus comps_amount. Sale discounts and comps are negative deductions because they reduce gross/net sales; negative values are not refunds or reversals by themselves.

meta.label: Discounts & comps
meta.tooltip: Combined discounts and comps applied to the order.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Net Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net sales excluding automatic gratuity (net = sales - returns). UK compliance metric. Equivalent to net_sales minus net auto_gratuity

meta.label: Net sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Net sales excluding service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: Orders.covered_net_sales_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Covered Net Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Covered Net Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net sales excluding automatic gratuity from orders with one or more covers. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Covered net sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers, excluding service charges payable to employees.
meta.context: Covered net sales (UK) only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0 and excludes service charges payable to employees. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover in UK reporting.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_per_cover_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Net Sales Per Cover Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Net Sales Per Cover Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Average net sales per cover excluding automatic gratuity, excluding orders without covers from the numerator. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Net sales per cover (UK)
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales excluding service charges payable to employees divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover (UK) is calculated as net sales from orders with covers, excluding service charges payable to employees, divided by total covers.

measures.name: Orders.sales_tax_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Sales Tax Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Sales tax excluding tax on automatic gratuity. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Sales tax (UK)
meta.tooltip: Sales tax excluding tax on service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_with_tax_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Net Sales with Tax Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Net Sales with Tax Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net sales with tax, excluding automatic gratuity and its tax (net = sales - returns). "Gross Sales" in UK and similar countries.

meta.label: Gross Sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Gross sales excluding service charges payable to employees and related tax for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: Orders.net_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Orders Net Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net automatic gratuity including tax (net = sales - returns + tax). UK compliance metric for total autogratuity collected.

meta.label: Service charges (Payable to Employees)
meta.tooltip: Net service charges payable to employees, including related tax, for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: Orders.tips_plus_net_auto_gratuity_sum
measures.title: Orders Tips Plus Net Auto Gratuity Sum
measures.shortTitle: Tips Plus Net Auto Gratuity Sum
measures.description: Tips plus net automatic gratuity, matching the UK Sales Summary payable-to-employees total.

meta.label: Tips & service charges
meta.tooltip: Total amount payable to employees, including tips and employee-payable service charges.

measures.name: Orders.top_line_product_sales_count
measures.title: Orders Top Line Product Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to top line product sales (gross sales)

meta.label: Gross sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with gross sales.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_count
measures.title: Orders Net Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to net sales

meta.label: Net sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with net sales.

measures.name: Orders.net_sales_with_tax_count
measures.title: Orders Net Sales with Tax Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to net sales with tax

meta.label: Net sales with tax order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with net sales including tax.

measures.name: Orders.item_sales_count
measures.title: Orders Item Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to item sales

meta.label: Item sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with item sales.

measures.name: Orders.total_sales_count
measures.title: Orders Total Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to total sales

meta.label: Total sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with total sales.

measures.name: Orders.subtotal_sales_count
measures.title: Orders Subtotal Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to subtotal sales

meta.label: Subtotal sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with subtotal sales.

measures.name: Orders.item_sales_without_exchanges_count
measures.title: Orders Item Sales Without Exchanges Count
measures.description: Number of orders with item sales excluding exchanges

meta.label: Item sales (no exchanges) order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with item sales excluding exchanges.

measures.name: Orders.item_sales_exchanges_count

dimensions.name: Orders.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Orders Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Orders.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Orders Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: Orders.local_date
dimensions.title: Orders Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Orders.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Orders Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Orders.local_month
dimensions.title: Orders Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Orders.local_day
dimensions.title: Orders Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Orders.local_hour
dimensions.title: Orders Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: Orders.local_minute
dimensions.title: Orders Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

meta.label: Revenue recognition timestamp
meta.tooltip: Timestamp when the sale is recognized for reporting based on the seller cash vs accrual setting. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

dimensions.description: Whether the order has been recognized for accrual-based reporting. Orders whose source accrual state is WILL_BE_RECOGNIZED are exposed here as RECOGNIZED once their service date has passed, and UNRECOGNIZED before then. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

meta.label: Accrual state
meta.tooltip: Whether the order has been recognized for accrual-based reporting yet.

dimensions.name: Orders.state
dimensions.title: Orders State
dimensions.description: The current state of the order (OPEN, COMPLETED, CANCELED, DRAFT)

meta.label: Order status
meta.tooltip: The status of the order.
meta.context: The status of the order. Can be open, completed, canceled, or draft.

dimensions.name: Orders.check_state
dimensions.title: Orders Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

segments.name: Orders.closed_checks
segments.title: Orders Closed Checks
segments.description: Only records from orders that are fully paid and settled. This is the recommended filter for standard sales reporting and matches the default behavior of Sales Summary reports.

segments.name: Orders.seated
segments.title: Orders Seated
segments.description: Orders assigned to a dining table on the restaurant floor plan. Use this (not fulfillment method) to identify dine-in / seated orders for table-turn reporting

segments.name: Orders.awaiting_capture
segments.title: Orders Awaiting Capture
segments.description: Only records from orders with payments received but not yet closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that are not closed out.

segments.name: Orders.open_checks
segments.title: Orders Open Checks
segments.description: Only records from orders that have not yet been paid for. These are open tabs or orders awaiting payment.

segments.name: Orders.fully_paid
segments.title: Orders Fully Paid
segments.description: Records from orders that have received payment, including both closed checks and checks awaiting capture. Excludes open/unpaid orders.

segments.name: Orders.no_tip
segments.title: Orders No Tip
segments.description: Orders with no tip amount

segments.name: Orders.has_tip
segments.title: Orders Has Tip
segments.description: Orders with a non-zero tip amount (includes negative tips from returns)

segments.name: Orders.has_customer
segments.title: Orders Has Customer
segments.description: Orders linked to an identified customer

segments.name: Orders.invoice_orders
segments.title: Orders Invoice Orders
segments.description: Only orders associated with an invoice

cubes.title: Orders

meta.label: Orders
meta.tooltip: Order-level sales data including totals, taxes, tips, discounts, and payment timing

examples.title: Largest Transactions
examples.description: What were my largest transactions this month?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Orders.order_id AS order_id,
  Orders.local_reporting_timestamp AS local_reporting_timestamp,
  Orders.local_date AS local_date,
  MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) AS top_line_product_sales
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
GROUP BY 1, 2, 3
ORDER BY MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Shows the highest-value transactions during the specified period. Uses closed_checks segment to filter to only fully paid and settled orders. Ordered by top_line_product_sales descending to show largest first. Filters on local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering. Includes local_date for timezone-aware date display (generated by local_time_dimensions macro at runtime).

examples.title: Busiest Weekend Hours
examples.description: When am I busiest on weekends this year?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Orders.local_date AS local_date,
  Orders.local_hour AS local_hour,
  MEASURE(Orders.count) AS count
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.local_day_of_week IN ('Saturday', 'Sunday')
  AND Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY MEASURE(Orders.count) DESC
LIMIT 50
examples.notes: Analyzes order volume by hour using local_hour dimension (converted to location's timezone). Uses timeDimensions for date filtering on local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

examples.title: Highest Sales by Employee
examples.description: Which employees had the highest sales last week?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Orders.team_member_attributed_to_name AS team_member_attributed_to_name,
  MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) AS top_line_product_sales
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 week'
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Shows gross sales by the team member attributed to the order — the employee Square considers responsible for the sale. 'Which employees had the highest sales' tilts toward attribution rather than collection. For sales grouped by who collected payment instead, use team_member_collected_by_name.

examples.title: Highest Sales by Attributed Employee
examples.description: Which attributed team members had the highest sales last week?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Orders.team_member_attributed_to_name AS team_member_attributed_to_name,
  MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) AS top_line_product_sales
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 week'
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) DESC
LIMIT 10
examples.notes: Shows gross sales by the team member attributed to the order — the employee Square considers responsible for the order, accounting for ownership transfers, ticket assignment, and appointment staff. Both this and highest-sales-by-employee use attribution. For sales grouped by who collected payment, use team_member_collected_by_name instead.

examples.title: Tips by Employee
examples.description: How much in tips did each employee collect this week?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Orders.team_member_collected_by_name AS team_member_collected_by_name,
  MEASURE(PaymentAndRefunds.tip_amount) AS tip_amount
FROM Orders
CROSS JOIN PaymentAndRefunds
WHERE PaymentAndRefunds.has_tip = TRUE
  AND PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 week'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(PaymentAndRefunds.tip_amount) DESC
examples.notes: Shows tips collected by the order's collector. team_member_collected_by_name resolves via Orders.team_member_collected_by_id (sourced from reporting.collectedBySingleton.id) to TeamMemberCollectedBy.full_name — the order-level rollup, not the per-payment swiper. NULL or MULTIPLE_EMPLOYEES rows are real and represent orders with no recorded collector or with multiple collectors.

examples.title: Transactions with No Tip
examples.description: How many transactions had no tip today?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MEASURE(Orders.count) AS count
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.no_tip = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 day'
examples.notes: Counts transactions where no tip was given using the no_tip segment. Uses closed_checks segment to ensure only completed transactions are counted. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

examples.title: Average Sales Per Transaction
examples.description: How does my 2025 average sales per transaction differ from the previous year?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('year', Orders.local_reporting_timestamp) AS year,
  MEASURE(Orders.avg_top_line_product_sales) AS avg_top_line_product_sales
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2024-01-01'
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
examples.notes: Calculates average gross sales (top line product sales) per order. The closed_checks segment ensures only completed orders are included in the calculation. The year granularity groups results into yearly buckets, enabling year-over-year comparison of average sales per transaction.

examples.title: Online vs. In-Store Sales Trend
examples.description: How have my online sales been trending vs. in-person from start of 2025 to end of October?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Channel.channel_category AS channel_category,
  Orders.local_reporting_timestamp AS local_reporting_timestamp,
  MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) AS top_line_product_sales,
  MEASURE(ItemTransactions.sales_quantity) AS sales_quantity
FROM Orders
CROSS JOIN Channel
CROSS JOIN ItemTransactions
WHERE Channel.channel_category IN ('ONLINE', 'IN-STORE')
  AND Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < '2025-11-01'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY Orders.local_reporting_timestamp ASC, Channel.channel_category ASC
examples.notes: Compares online and in-store sales trends over time by channel category. Uses a join to the Channel cube to access channel_category dimension. Includes both gross sales (top_line_product_sales) and item quantity metrics. The month granularity groups results into monthly buckets, enabling month-over-month trend analysis. Results are ordered by time ascending for chronological visualization. The closed_checks segment ensures only completed orders are counted. Note: This query also joins to ItemTransactions cube for quantity metrics.

examples.title: High-Value Customers
examples.description: Which customers spent more than $200 this quarter?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  CustomerSnapshots.customer_id AS customer_id,
  MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) AS top_line_product_sales
FROM Orders
CROSS JOIN CustomerSnapshots
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND CustomerSnapshots.customer_id IS NOT NULL
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '3 months'
GROUP BY 1
HAVING MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) > 200
examples.notes: Identifies customers who have spent more than $200 during a specific date range. Uses CustomerSnapshots join to access customer_id, with 'set' operator filter to ensure only orders with an associated customer are included. Filters Orders.top_line_product_sales > 200 to find high-value transactions. The closed_checks segment ensures only completed transactions are counted. Uses Orders.local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering.

examples.title: Sales by Location
examples.description: What are my sales by location over the last 30 days?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Location.location_name AS location_name,
  MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) AS top_line_product_sales
FROM Orders
CROSS JOIN Location
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '1 day'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(Orders.top_line_product_sales) DESC
examples.notes: Shows sales grouped by location name. When 'sales' is ambiguous (not specified as net, total, etc.), default to gross sales (top_line_product_sales). Uses the Location join to access location_name dimension for human-readable grouping. The closed_checks segment ensures only completed orders are counted. Results ordered by sales descending to show highest-performing locations first. Uses a rolling 30-day window for recent performance analysis.

examples.title: Tax by Destination Postal Code
examples.description: What's my sales tax this month broken down by destination postal code and state? (For state-tax filing.)
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Orders.tax_destination_state AS tax_destination_state,
  Orders.tax_destination_postal_code AS tax_destination_postal_code,
  MEASURE(Orders.sales_tax_amount) AS sales_tax_amount
FROM Orders
WHERE Orders.closed_checks = TRUE
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND Orders.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY MEASURE(Orders.sales_tax_amount) DESC
LIMIT 50
examples.notes: Exercises the tax destination postal code/state dimensions for state-tax-filing use cases. Shipment and delivery orders use the buyer destination; other orders use the merchant location. Orders with multiple shipment or delivery fulfillments return 'MULTIPLE_POSTAL_CODES'/'MULTIPLE_STATES'. minRows=0 because a quiet test merchant may have no closed orders this month.

cubes.description: Top level Order data (sales and returns), like top line product sales, net sales, etc. that you would see in something like a Sales Summary report. Includes orders in all check states (open, awaiting capture, and closed). IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, use the 'closed_checks' segment to filter to only fully paid and settled orders and filter/group by local_reporting_timestamp or its local_* dimensions. Use created_at only when the user explicitly asks when orders were created or placed. Use 'open_checks' or 'awaiting_capture' segments only if you explicitly need to analyze orders in those states. The cube excludes house account invoices, non-reportable orders, and failed/canceled/draft orders.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: For payments processed by Square, this field will initially be `null`, and will get set depending on the type of payment method It will be set based on the following conditions: - Card and Payments Modeled as Card (Afterpay, PayPay, etc ): Set to the record's `authIntent.createdAt` - Square Gift Cards: Set to the record's `captureIntent.createdAt` - Bank Transfers: Set to the record's `bankTransaction.completedAt` - Async Transaction: Set to the record's `asyncTransaction.completedAt` On failed payments, this field will remain unset permanently For payments recorded but not processed by Square (which can never fail, and have no processing lifecycle), this field will always be set, typically to the `createdAt` timestamp For refunds, this will be created at time of the refund

meta.label: Payment timestamp
meta.tooltip: Time when the payment or refund was recorded.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_date
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_month
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_day
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_hour
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_minute
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: PaymentAndRefunds.local_year
dimensions.title: Payment and Refunds Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

cubes.title: Payment and Refunds

meta.label: Payments & Refunds
meta.tooltip: Payment and refund transactions with amounts, methods, and status

examples.title: Transactions with No Tip
examples.description: How many transactions had no tip yesterday?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  MEASURE(PaymentAndRefunds.orders_count) AS orders_count
FROM PaymentAndRefunds
WHERE PaymentAndRefunds.no_tip = TRUE
  AND PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 day'
  AND PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp < CURRENT_DATE
examples.notes: Counts the number of distinct orders that had no tip yesterday. Uses the no_tip segment which filters for PAYMENT type transactions where payment_tip_money.amount is either NULL or 0. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering based on when the payment was processed by Square.

examples.title: Tips by Location
examples.description: How much did I collect in tips at each location?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  Location.location_name AS location_name,
  MEASURE(PaymentAndRefunds.tip_amount) AS tip_amount
FROM PaymentAndRefunds
CROSS JOIN Location
WHERE PaymentAndRefunds.has_tip = TRUE
  AND PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND PaymentAndRefunds.local_reporting_timestamp < '2025-07-01'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY MEASURE(PaymentAndRefunds.tip_amount) DESC
examples.notes: Shows total tips collected grouped by location name. Uses the payments segment to filter to only payment transactions (tips don't apply to refunds). Uses the Location join to access location_name dimension for human-readable grouping. Results ordered by tip_amount descending to show highest-tipping locations first. Uses local_reporting_timestamp for timezone-aware filtering based on when the payment was processed.

cubes.description: Top level Payments and Refunds data, including itemized payment amounts and tip amounts. Likely to see this in something like Payment Methods report. Notably only includes Payments and Refunds on OPEN or COMPLETED Orders; excludes fraud, no‑sale, offline failed card attempts, CANCELED/DRAFT/FAILED Orders, CANCELED/FAILED payments and FAILED/REJECTED refunds

dimensions.name: ServiceCharges.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ServiceCharges.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: ServiceCharges.local_hour

dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: ServiceCharges.check_state
dimensions.title: Service Charges Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

segments.name: ServiceCharges.fully_paid
segments.title: Service Charges Fully Paid
segments.description: Records from orders that have received payment, including both closed checks and checks awaiting capture. Excludes open/unpaid orders.

cubes.title: Service Charges

meta.label: Service Charges
meta.tooltip: Service charges applied to orders, including auto-gratuity and card surcharges

cubes.description: Service charges applied to orders, including custom service charges, card surcharges, and automatic gratuity. Includes both sale service charges and returned service charges. Each row represents one service charge on an order or return. Service charges are fees added to orders beyond the base item prices, such as delivery fees, processing fees, or automatic gratuity for large parties. Includes both the service charge amount and any taxes applied to the service charge.

meta.label: Shifts

cubes.description: Shift records that link timecards (actual worked time) to shift schedules (planned shifts). Each shift represents the connection between what was scheduled and what was actually worked. A shift may have a timecard (worked), a shift schedule (planned), both (scheduled and worked), or neither. Used to reconcile planned vs actual labor, track schedule adherence, and analyze shift coverage

cubes.title: Team Member Core

meta.label: Team Members
meta.tooltip: Your team member directory with contact details, roles, and status

examples.title: Team Members Sorted by Last Name
examples.description: List team members showing the full name on each row, sorted alphabetically by last name
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  TeamMemberCore.team_member_id AS team_member_id,
  TeamMemberCore.full_name AS full_name,
  MEASURE(TeamMemberCore.first_name) AS first_name,
  MEASURE(TeamMemberCore.last_name) AS last_name
FROM TeamMemberCore
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY MEASURE(TeamMemberCore.last_name) ASC
examples.notes: Shows the string measures first_name and last_name as row-level values next to the full_name dimension. Grouping by team_member_id keeps the rows at team-member grain even when multiple team members share a full name. Sorting on the last_name measure orders rows by last name while each row still displays the full name. Null values indicate team members without a recorded name.

measures.name: Timecard.count
measures.title: Timecard Count
measures.description: Total number of timecard entries

measures.title: Timecard Unique Employees
measures.description: Number of unique team members who worked shifts

measures.name: Timecard.total_declared_tip
measures.title: Timecard Total Declared Tip
measures.description: Total amount of tips declared by team members across timecards

measures.name: Timecard.total_hours_worked
measures.title: Timecard Total Hours Worked
measures.description: Estimated total hours worked across all closed timecards. Only includes timecards where the team member has clocked out. This is a straight-time estimate and does not account for overtime rules, doubletime, workweek configurations, break adjustments, or state-specific labor rules.

measures.name: Timecard.total_labor_cost
measures.title: Timecard Total Labor Cost
measures.description: Estimated total labor cost across all closed timecards with an hourly wage, calculated as hours worked multiplied by the straight-time hourly rate. Excludes timecards with no wage (salaried employees or unset wages). This is an estimate and does not account for overtime/doubletime multipliers, workweek configurations, break adjustments, or state-specific labor rules.

meta.label: Total labor cost
meta.tooltip: The labor cost is an estimate based on hours worked and base wage, and does not take into account factors like overtime rules and state-specific premiums.

measures.name: Timecard.avg_hourly_wage
measures.title: Timecard Avg Hourly Wage
measures.description: Average hourly wage rate across closed timecards with an hourly wage. Excludes open timecards and timecards with no wage (salaried employees or unset wages).

cubes.description: Timecard entries tracking when team members clock in and out for work shifts. Each timecard represents a work session for a team member, including the job role they worked, their hourly wage at that time, location where they worked, and any declared cash tips. Used for hours worked calculations, labor cost analysis, and payroll processing. Only includes non-deleted timecards.
