dimensions.description: Sale timestamp, roughly based on cash accounting. Set to the time the final payments were received.

meta.label: Sale timestamp
meta.tooltip: Date and time when the transaction occurred.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Voids Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Voids Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: Voids.local_date
dimensions.title: Voids Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Voids Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_month
dimensions.title: Voids Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_day
dimensions.title: Voids Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_hour
dimensions.title: Voids Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_minute
dimensions.title: Voids Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_year
dimensions.title: Voids Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: Voids.revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Voids Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Timestamp when this row's sale is recognized for reporting based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting. Cash/default uses the existing payment reporting timestamp; accrual uses the service-date reporting timestamp. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Revenue recognition timestamp
meta.tooltip: Timestamp when the sale is recognized for reporting based on the seller cash vs accrual setting. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: Voids.revenue_recognition_state
dimensions.title: Voids Revenue Recognition State
dimensions.description: Whether this row's sale is recognized, projected to be recognized later, or not considered a sale according to the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Revenue recognition state
meta.tooltip: Whether this row is recognized for revenue reporting based on the seller cash vs accrual setting. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp

dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.title: Voids Local Revenue Recognition Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_revenue_recognition_hour
dimensions.title: Voids Local Revenue Recognition Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_revenue_recognition_minute
dimensions.title: Voids Local Revenue Recognition Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: Voids.local_revenue_recognition_year
dimensions.title: Voids Local Revenue Recognition Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: Voids.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Voids Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: Voids.check_state
dimensions.title: Voids Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

segments.name: Voids.closed_checks
segments.title: Voids Closed Checks
segments.description: Only records from orders that are fully paid and settled. This is the recommended filter for standard sales reporting and matches the default behavior of Sales Summary reports.

segments.name: Voids.awaiting_capture
segments.title: Voids Awaiting Capture
segments.description: Only records from orders with payments received but not yet closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that are not closed out.

segments.name: Voids.open_checks
segments.title: Voids Open Checks
segments.description: Only records from orders that have not yet been paid for. These are open tabs or orders awaiting payment.

segments.name: Voids.fully_paid
segments.title: Voids Fully Paid
segments.description: Records from orders that have received payment, including both closed checks and checks awaiting capture. Excludes open/unpaid orders.

cubes.title: Voids

meta.label: Voids
meta.tooltip: Voided items and orders with reasons and amounts

cubes.description: Item-level snapshots carried inside order void events. Each row represents one embedded item from `orders.voids[*].lineItems`, so item attributes come from the void payload itself rather than from `orders.lineItems`. This cube is intentionally item-grain even when a single void event affects multiple items.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.total_current_cash_balance
measures.title: Cash Drawers Total Current Cash Balance
measures.description: Total current cash drawer balance across selected cash drawer shifts in main currency units.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.total_cash_tender_payments
measures.title: Cash Drawers Total Cash Tender Payments
measures.description: Total cash tender payment amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.total_cash_tender_cancelled_payments
measures.title: Cash Drawers Total Cash Tender Cancelled Payments
measures.description: Total cancelled cash tender payment amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.total_cash_tender_refunds
measures.title: Cash Drawers Total Cash Tender Refunds
measures.description: Total cash tender refund amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.total_paid_in
measures.title: Cash Drawers Total Paid in
measures.description: Total paid-in amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.total_paid_out
measures.title: Cash Drawers Total Paid out
measures.description: Total paid-out amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Returned as a positive amount.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.net_cash_movement
measures.title: Cash Drawers Net Cash Movement
measures.description: Net cash movement amount across cash drawer shifts in main currency units. Cash tender payments and paid-in events are positive; cancelled cash tender payments, cash tender refunds, and paid-out events are negative.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.expected_cash
measures.title: Cash Drawers Expected Cash
measures.description: Expected cash amount in main currency units, calculated as starting cash plus cash tender payments and paid in, minus cancelled cash tender payments, cash tender refunds, and paid out.

measures.name: CashDrawerReport.cash_over_short
measures.title: Cash Drawers Cash over Short
measures.description: Cash over/short amount in main currency units, calculated as closed cash minus expected cash for shifts with a closed cash amount.

cubes.description: Curated view for cash drawer reporting. Reports one row per cash drawer shift with pre-aggregated cash movement totals including starting cash, closed cash, paid in, paid out, expected cash, and cash over/short. measures.name: CoItemSalesReport.pair_orders_count

measures.description: Count of paid orders matching the row. For item pairs, counts orders containing both items. For rows where the item sold alone, counts orders where it was the only item sold.

meta.percentage_tooltip: Share of orders with the main item that also included this item. For rows where the item sold by itself, shows the share of orders for the main item where nothing else appeared on the order.
meta.label: Orders sold together
meta.tooltip: Paid orders that included this combination of items. For rows where the item sold by itself, the count is how many orders contained only that item.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Items sold together Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_date
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_month
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_day
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_hour
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: CoItemSalesReport.local_minute
dimensions.title: Items sold together Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

measures.name: DiscountsAndComps.discounted_quantity

measures.description: Net quantity on line items with a discount applied (sales positive, returns negative)

meta.label: Discounted quantity
meta.tooltip: Net quantity on line items with a discount applied. Includes returns.
meta.context: The net quantity on line items with a discount applied. Sales are positive and returns are negative. If multiple discounts are applied to the same line item, its quantity is counted once for each discount application.

measures.name: DiscountsAndComps.discounts_applied_count

measures.description: Count of distinct discounts applied

meta.tooltip: Number of discounts applied.

measures.name: DiscountsAndComps.discount_amount
measures.title: Discounts & Comps Discount Amount
measures.description: Item-level net discount amount. Sale discounts are positive and return discounts are negative. This differs from Sales.discounts_amount / Orders.discounts_amount, where sale discounts are negative Sales Summary deductions.

meta.label: Discount amount
meta.tooltip: Amount of money in discounts applied. Includes returns.
meta.context: Amount of money in discounts applied. Includes returns and does not include comps. Positive sale discount amounts are real discounts. This sign convention differs from Sales.discounts_amount and Orders.discounts_amount, where sale discounts are negative because they reduce Sales Summary totals.

measures.name: ItemSales.net_quantity

meta.tooltip: Net quantity of items (sales minus returns). For raw units sold without returns netted, use Units sold.

measures.name: ItemSales.item_combo_sold

meta.label: Combo units sold
meta.tooltip: Number of combo units sold.
meta.context: The number of combo units sold. Only counts line items that are part of a combo or line item group.

measures.name: ItemSales.combos_sold_count
measures.title: Item Sales Combos Sold Count
measures.description: Number of combos sold, correctly accounting for coalesced combo quantities (e.g., 3x of the same combo counts as 3, not 1). Accurate when grouped by combo name, date, location, or other combo/order-level dimensions. May be inaccurate when grouped by individual item name, since the combo quantity is distributed across component items and rounded after aggregation.

meta.label: Combos sold
meta.tooltip: Number of combos sold. Accurate when grouped by combo, date, or location. May be approximate when grouped by individual item.
meta.context: The number of combos sold. The combo quantity is distributed across component items and rounded post-aggregation, so results are exact for combo-level or higher groupings but may be approximate when grouped by individual item name.

measures.name: ItemSales.item_combo_gross_sales

meta.label: Combo sales

meta.label: Net item sales

meta.context: Amount of item gross sales minus returns. Sales are positive and returns are negative.

measures.name: ItemSales.item_discount_money
measures.title: Item Sales Item Discount Money

meta.context: Total amount of discounts applied to items. Positive values on sale rows are real discounts; returns are negative. This sign convention differs from Sales.discounts_amount and Orders.discounts_amount, where sale discounts are negative because they are Sales Summary deductions.

measures.name: ItemSales.item_comps_money

measures.name: ItemSales.comps_amount
measures.title: Item Sales Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Item Sales Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_minute
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_hour
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_date
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_month
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ItemSales.local_year
dimensions.title: Item Sales Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

examples.title: Monthly Item Sales
examples.description: What are my item sales by month for a given period?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', ItemSales.local_reporting_timestamp) AS month,
  ItemSales.item_name AS item_name,
  MEASURE(ItemSales.sales_gross_amount) AS gross_sales,
  MEASURE(ItemSales.items_sold_count) AS quantity_sold
FROM ItemSales
WHERE ItemSales.transacted_at >= '2025-01-01'
  AND ItemSales.transacted_at < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1, 2
ORDER BY month, gross_sales DESC
examples.notes: Use DATE_TRUNC on localized timestamp to bucket by time period. All measures must be wrapped in MEASURE(). Use >= / < date comparisons for filtering — YEAR() and EXTRACT() do not work in Cube SQL. Every dimension in SELECT must appear in GROUP BY.

examples.title: Category Sales Share
examples.description: What share of gross sales does each category represent?
examples.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    ItemSales.category_name AS category,
    MEASURE(ItemSales.sales_gross_amount) AS gross_sales
  FROM ItemSales
  WHERE ItemSales.transacted_at >= '2025-01-01'
    AND ItemSales.transacted_at < '2026-01-01'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  category,
  gross_sales,
  ROUND(100.0 * gross_sales / SUM(gross_sales) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total
FROM base
ORDER BY gross_sales DESC
examples.notes: Inner query uses restricted Cube SQL: groups by existing dimensions and uses MEASURE() for aggregations. Outer query uses standard SQL (window functions, arithmetic) for derived computations like percentages.

cubes.description: Curated view for item sales reporting. Reports only on closed (fully settled) orders and includes item transaction data with related discounts, modifiers, and order-level metrics. Use this view for item sales reports with a filtered set of dimensions and measures relevant to item sales analysis.

cubes.name: KDS

measures.name: KDS.ticket_count
measures.title: Kds Ticket Count
measures.description: Count of kitchen tickets (chits per station). Each ticket represents one station's view of a kitchen order. The same order may produce multiple tickets if it is routed to multiple stations (e.g., Prep then Expo). Exposed as 'ticket_count' on the KDS view and 'kds_ticket_count' on the SpeedOfService view

meta.label: Kitchen tickets
meta.tooltip: Total number of kitchen tickets (one per chit per station).

measures.name: KDS.total_line_items
measures.title: Kds Total Line Items
measures.description: Total item quantity across all tickets

meta.label: Total items
meta.tooltip: Total item quantity across all tickets.

measures.name: KDS.avg_line_items_per_ticket
measures.title: Kds Avg Line Items Per Ticket
measures.description: Average item quantity per ticket

meta.label: Avg items per ticket
meta.tooltip: Average item quantity per ticket.

measures.name: KDS.avg_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Avg Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Average (mean) time in seconds from when a ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when the last item on that ticket was marked complete. WARNING: heavily skewed by outlier tickets that were left open for hours (e.g., never marked done at end of shift). For 'typical' or representative completion time, prefer median_ticket_time_seconds. Group by station_type to compare Prep vs. Expo performance, or join to DeviceCodes and group by DeviceCodes.device_code_name to compare individual stations

meta.label: Avg ticket time
meta.tooltip: Average seconds from ticket display on the KDS screen to completion of the last item. Skewed by outliers — prefer median for typical completion time.
meta.context: Measures how long a station takes to complete all items on a ticket. The clock starts when the ticket first appears on the station's screen (display_on_kds_at) and stops when the last line item is marked done (actual_completed_at). The mean is sensitive to outlier tickets — tickets occasionally remain open for hours and inflate the mean by orders of magnitude. For seller-facing 'typical time' questions, prefer median_ticket_time_seconds, which is robust to outliers without imposing an arbitrary cutoff. To identify slow stations, group this measure by DeviceCodes.device_code_name or station_type.

measures.name: KDS.min_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Min Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Minimum actual time in seconds from ticket display to completion

meta.label: Min prep time
meta.tooltip: Minimum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: KDS.max_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Max Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Maximum actual time in seconds from ticket display to completion

meta.label: Max prep time
meta.tooltip: Maximum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: KDS.median_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Median Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Median time in seconds from when a ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when the last item on that ticket was marked complete. More robust than average for identifying typical station performance because it is not skewed by outlier tickets

meta.label: Median ticket time
meta.tooltip: Median seconds from ticket display on the KDS screen to completion of the last item.

measures.name: KDS.avg_time_due_seconds
measures.title: Kds Avg Time Due Seconds
measures.description: Average time due in seconds from ticket display (only for tickets with a time due)

meta.label: Avg target time
meta.tooltip: Average target completion time in seconds.

measures.name: KDS.avg_time_vs_due_seconds
measures.title: Kds Avg Time Vs Due Seconds
measures.description: Average difference in seconds between actual completion and time due (positive = late, negative = early, 0 if no time due)

meta.label: Avg time vs target
meta.tooltip: Average difference in seconds between completion and target time.
meta.context: Positive values indicate late completion, negative values indicate early completion. Zero if no target time was set.

measures.name: KDS.tickets_with_time_due
measures.title: Kds Tickets with Time Due

meta.label: Tickets with target time

measures.name: KDS.items_count
measures.title: Kds Items Count
measures.description: Number of line items (rows). Each row is one line item on one station's kitchen ticket. A line item with quantity 3 is one row (count=1, quantity_sold=3) — use quantity_sold to sum units. The same line item routed through multiple stations (prep + expo) produces multiple rows. Use this for discrete line-item questions (e.g., recalls, % recalled). Exposed as 'items_count' on the KDS view.

meta.label: Line items
meta.tooltip: Number of line items (rows) on kitchen tickets.

measures.name: KDS.quantity_sold
measures.title: Kds Quantity Sold
measures.description: Total quantity of items

meta.label: Total quantity
meta.tooltip: Total quantity of items prepared.

measures.name: KDS.avg_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Avg Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Average (mean) time in seconds from when the ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when this specific item was marked complete. Use this to identify which items take longest to prepare. Group by item_name and station_type to see how item prep times vary across station roles. WARNING: heavily skewed by outlier items that were left on open tickets for hours. For "typical" or representative item completion time, prefer median_item_time_seconds.

meta.label: Avg item time
meta.tooltip: Average seconds from KDS display to item completion. Prefer median if outliers are a concern.
meta.context: Measures how long an individual item takes from the moment its ticket appears on a station's screen until the item is marked done. Unlike the ticket-level avg_ticket_time_seconds (in KitchenPerformanceTickets), this measures each item independently — useful for identifying slow-cooking items or items that bottleneck a station. For seller-facing "typical time" questions, prefer median_item_time_seconds because the mean can be heavily skewed by items that were left on tickets that stayed open for hours.

measures.name: KDS.min_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Min Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Minimum time in seconds from ticket display to item completion

meta.label: Min prep time
meta.tooltip: Minimum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: KDS.max_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Max Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Maximum time in seconds from ticket display to item completion

meta.label: Max prep time
meta.tooltip: Maximum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: KDS.median_item_time_seconds
measures.title: Kds Median Item Time Seconds
measures.description: Median time in seconds from when the ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when this specific item was marked complete. More robust than average for identifying typical item prep times because it is not skewed by outlier items

meta.label: Median item time
meta.tooltip: Median seconds from ticket display on the KDS screen to this item being marked complete.

measures.name: KDS.item_avg_time_vs_due_seconds
measures.title: Kds Item Avg Time Vs Due Seconds
measures.shortTitle: Item Avg Time Vs Due Seconds
measures.description: Average difference in seconds between item completion and time due (positive = late, negative = early, 0 if no time due)

meta.label: Avg time vs target
meta.tooltip: Average difference in seconds between completion and target time.
meta.context: Positive values indicate late completion, negative values indicate early completion. Zero if no target time was set.

measures.name: KDS.item_quantity_with_time_due
measures.title: Kds Item Quantity with Time Due
measures.description: Total quantity of items from tickets that have a time due

meta.label: Items with target time
meta.tooltip: Quantity of items with a target completion time.

measures.name: KDS.item_quantity_completed_late
measures.title: Kds Item Quantity Completed Late
measures.description: Total quantity of items (with a time due) completed after that time

meta.label: Late items
meta.tooltip: Quantity of items completed after their target time.

measures.name: KDS.item_percent_late
measures.title: Kds Item Percent Late
measures.description: Percentage of item quantity (with a time due) that was completed late

meta.label: Percent late
meta.tooltip: Percentage of items completed after their target time.

measures.name: KDS.recalled_quantity
measures.title: Kds Recalled Quantity
measures.description: Total summed quantity of recalled items. Because quantity is a DOUBLE, fractional units (e.g. bananas sold by weight) inflate this measure beyond a simple count of recall events. Prefer recall_count when a seller asks "how many items were recalled?" and prefer recall_rate when they ask for a percentage. Use recalled_quantity only when the seller explicitly cares about summed units (e.g., "how many recalled units did we ring up?").

meta.label: Recalled items
meta.tooltip: Total summed quantity of items that were recalled.

measures.name: KDS.recall_count
measures.title: Kds Recall Count
measures.description: Number of line items that were recalled. Each row counts as 1 regardless of quantity, so this is the right measure for "how many items were recalled?", "count of recalls", or for the numerator of a recall rate. Differs from recalled_quantity, which sums the quantity column and therefore inflates for items priced by weight or with quantity > 1.

meta.label: Recall count
meta.tooltip: Number of line items that were recalled.

measures.name: KDS.recall_rate
measures.title: Kds Recall Rate
measures.description: Percentage of line items that were recalled, returned as a number between 0 and 100. Use this for "what is the recall rate?", "% recalled", or "% of items remade" questions. Computed as 100 * recall_count / line_item_count, so both numerator and denominator are row counts (not quantity sums). Group by station_type, device_code_name, or item_name to compare recall rates across stations or items.

meta.label: Recall rate
meta.tooltip: Percentage of line items that were recalled.

meta.tooltip: Identifier for the associated order.

dimensions.name: KDS.order_source

meta.label: Order source
meta.tooltip: Where the order originated.

dimensions.name: KDS.station_type
dimensions.title: Kds Station Type
dimensions.description: The role of the kitchen station that processed this ticket. `kds` stations are prep/make-line stations where food and drinks are prepared (e.g., a 'Grill' station or 'Hot Drinks' station). `kds_expo` stations are expeditor stations where completed items are reviewed and assembled before being sent to the customer. To see the specific station name (e.g., 'Hot Drinks'), join to DeviceCodes and use DeviceCodes.device_code_name

meta.label: Station type
meta.tooltip: The role of the kitchen station: Prep (where items are made) or Expo (where orders are assembled and reviewed before serving).
meta.context: Stations are the individual KDS screens in a kitchen, each assigned a role. `kds`: A preparation/make-line station where kitchen staff make specific items (e.g., a grill station, a drinks station). `kds_expo`: An expeditor station that reviews and assembles completed items from prep stations before they go to the customer. A single kitchen can have many `kds` (prep) stations but typically one or two `kds_expo` stations. To compare performance between individual stations (not just station types), join to the DeviceCodes cube and group by DeviceCodes.device_code_name.

dimensions.name: KDS.device_id
dimensions.title: Kds Device Id
dimensions.description: The device code ID of the KDS station that processed this ticket. Despite the name, this is a device code identifier (not a physical device ID). Each device code represents a named station in the kitchen (e.g., 'Hot Drinks', 'Grill'). Join to the DeviceCodes cube to get the human-readable station name (DeviceCodes.device_code_name) or to the Devices cube (via DeviceCodes) for physical device details

meta.label: Station device code ID
meta.tooltip: The device code ID identifying which kitchen station processed this ticket. Join to DeviceCodes for the station name.
meta.context: This field contains the device code ID, which is the unique identifier for a named KDS station. Sellers create device codes in Square Dashboard under Settings > Device Management > Device Codes, where they assign a name (like 'Hot Drinks' or 'Grill') and a role (Prep or Expo). To get the station name, join this cube to DeviceCodes using device_id = DeviceCodes.device_code_id, then use DeviceCodes.device_code_name.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Kds Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_reporting_timestamp_str
dimensions.title: Kds Local Reporting Timestamp Str
dimensions.description: Full timestamp in YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss format in the location's local timezone, as a string. You can use this dimension if you need to specify a query timezone (but since it's not a `time` type, you have to use it like a string and not a time dimension).

dimensions.name: KDS.local_date
dimensions.title: Kds Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Kds Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_month
dimensions.title: Kds Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_day
dimensions.title: Kds Local Day
dimensions.description: Day of month (1-31) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_hour
dimensions.title: Kds Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_minute
dimensions.title: Kds Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.local_year
dimensions.title: Kds Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: KDS.merchant_name
dimensions.title: Kds Merchant Name
dimensions.description: Display name of the merchant

meta.label: Merchant name
meta.tooltip: Display name of the merchant.

dimensions.name: KDS.item_name
dimensions.title: Kds Item Name
dimensions.description: Name of the menu item as it appears on the KDS ticket (e.g., 'Cheeseburger', 'Latte'). This is the item's display name, not a product category or classification

dimensions.name: KDS.device_code_name

meta.label: Station / device name

meta.context: Sellers assign this name when creating a device code in Square Dashboard under Settings > Device Management > Device Codes. For kitchen reporting, this is the human-readable station name. Use this dimension with KitchenPerformanceTickets to compare performance across individual stations (e.g., 'Which station has the longest average ticket time?').

dimensions.name: KDS.application_type

meta.context: KDS: Kitchen Display System — a kitchen station screen. RESTAURANT: Square for Restaurants POS. SQUARE_POS: Square Point of Sale. FNB_KIOSK: Food & Beverage self-service kiosk. TERMINAL_API: Square Terminal via API integration. Filter to KDS to see only kitchen stations.

meta.label: Kitchen Display

catalog.label: Kitchen
catalog.tooltip: Kitchen ticket and item performance

presets.label: Kitchen Tickets
presets.tooltip: Ticket times and on-time rates by station

presets.label: Kitchen Items
presets.tooltip: Prep time, quantities, and recalls by item

cubes.description: Unified Kitchen Display System (KDS) view combining ticket-level performance, item-level performance, location details, and station/device information. Use this view for comprehensive KDS reporting without needing to manually join cubes. Contains measures for kitchen ticket counts, prep/completion times, on-time vs late percentages, item-level timing, and item recall tracking. Supports grouping by location, station type, device, time of day, day of week, item name, item variation, course, and ticket size (item quantity on the ticket).

cubes.name: Labor

measures.name: Labor.count
measures.title: Labor Count
measures.description: Total number of timecard entries

measures.name: Labor.total_hours_worked
measures.title: Labor Total Hours Worked
measures.description: Estimated total hours worked across all closed timecards. Only includes timecards where the team member has clocked out. This is a straight-time estimate and does not account for overtime rules, doubletime, workweek configurations, break adjustments, or state-specific labor rules.

measures.name: Labor.total_labor_cost
measures.title: Labor Total Labor Cost
measures.description: Estimated total labor cost across all closed timecards with an hourly wage, calculated as hours worked multiplied by the straight-time hourly rate. Excludes timecards with no wage (salaried employees or unset wages). This is an estimate and does not account for overtime/doubletime multipliers, workweek configurations, break adjustments, or state-specific labor rules.

meta.label: Total labor cost
meta.tooltip: The labor cost is an estimate based on hours worked and base wage, and does not take into account factors like overtime rules and state-specific premiums.

measures.name: Labor.unique_employees
measures.title: Labor Unique Employees
measures.description: Number of unique team members who worked shifts

measures.name: Labor.avg_hourly_wage
measures.title: Labor Avg Hourly Wage
measures.description: Average hourly wage rate across closed timecards with an hourly wage. Excludes open timecards and timecards with no wage (salaried employees or unset wages).

measures.name: Labor.total_declared_tip
measures.title: Labor Total Declared Tip
measures.description: Total amount of tips declared by team members across timecards

measures.name: Labor.first_name
measures.title: Labor First Name

meta.label: First name
meta.tooltip: First name of the team member.

measures.name: Labor.last_name
measures.title: Labor Last Name
measures.description: The last name of the team member. Returns MULTIPLE_TEAM_MEMBERS when the aggregation contains more than one team member

meta.label: Last name
meta.tooltip: Last name of the team member.

measures.name: Labor.avg_hourly_rate
measures.title: Labor Avg Hourly Rate
measures.description: Average configured hourly pay rate across job assignments. Assignments with no configured hourly rate are excluded, so unset rates do not pull the average toward zero

meta.label: Average job pay rate
meta.tooltip: Average configured job pay rate across job assignments. Assignments with no configured rate are excluded.

measures.name: LaborSales.labor_hours
measures.title: Labor Sales Labor Hours

meta.label: Labor hours
meta.tooltip: Estimated hours worked from closed timecards.

measures.name: LaborSales.labor_cost
measures.title: Labor Sales Labor Cost
measures.description: Estimated labor cost from closed timecards, calculated as straight-time hours worked multiplied by the base hourly wage. Excludes timecards with no wage and does not account for overtime, doubletime, breaks, premiums, or state-specific labor rules.

meta.label: Labor cost
meta.tooltip: Estimated labor cost based on hours worked and base wage. It does not account for overtime rules or state-specific premiums.

measures.name: LaborSales.sales_per_labor_hour
measures.title: Labor Sales Sales Per Labor Hour
measures.description: Net sales divided by estimated labor hours. This is a ratio of sums, so it remains valid when grouped by shared dimensions such as location, date, hour, daypart, or team member.

meta.label: Sales per labor hour
meta.tooltip: Net sales divided by estimated labor hours.

measures.name: LaborSales.labor_cost_per_hour
measures.title: Labor Sales Labor Cost Per Hour
measures.description: Estimated labor cost divided by estimated labor hours

meta.label: Labor cost per hour
meta.tooltip: Estimated labor cost divided by estimated labor hours.

measures.name: LaborSales.labor_cost_percent_of_sales
measures.title: Labor Sales Labor Cost Percent of Sales
measures.description: Estimated labor cost divided by net sales

meta.label: Labor cost % of sales
meta.tooltip: Estimated labor cost as a percentage of net sales.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Merchant Id
dimensions.description: The merchant ID for the sales or labor record

dimensions.name: LaborSales.location_id
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Location Id
dimensions.description: Location where the sale occurred or where the team member clocked in

dimensions.name: LaborSales.location_name
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Location Name
dimensions.description: Display name of the location where the sale occurred or where the team member clocked in

dimensions.name: LaborSales.location_timezone
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Location Timezone
dimensions.description: Time zone for the location

dimensions.name: LaborSales.metric_source
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Metric Source
dimensions.description: Whether this row contributes sales metrics or labor metrics

dimensions.name: LaborSales.currency
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Currency
dimensions.description: Currency for the monetary sales or labor values on this row

dimensions.name: LaborSales.activity_timestamp
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Activity Timestamp
dimensions.description: Timestamp used to align sales and labor. For sales, this is the order reporting timestamp. For labor, this is the timecard clock-in timestamp.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_minute
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_hour
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_date
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_month
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.local_year
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.daypart
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Daypart
dimensions.description: Simple local-time daypart bucket derived from the sale timestamp or timecard clock-in timestamp

meta.label: Daypart
meta.tooltip: Daypart based on local sale time or timecard clock-in time.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.team_member_id
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Team Member Id
dimensions.description: Team member associated with the row. For sales, this is the order-attributed team member. For labor, this is the employee who worked the timecard.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.team_member_name
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Team Member Name
dimensions.description: Team member name associated with the row. For sales, this is the order-attributed team member. For labor, this is the employee who worked the timecard.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.job_title
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Job Title
dimensions.description: The job title for labor rows, based on the timecard role worked. Sales rows do not carry job attribution.

dimensions.name: LaborSales.merchant_name
dimensions.title: Labor Sales Merchant Name
dimensions.description: Display name of the merchant

meta.label: Merchant name
meta.tooltip: Display name of the merchant.

dimensions.name: ModifierSales.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Modifier Sales Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ModifierSales.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Modifier Sales Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ModifierSales.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Modifier Sales Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: ModifierSales.local_date

dimensions.name: ModifierSales.local_hour
dimensions.title: Modifier Sales Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

presets.label: Modifiers

cubes.description: Curated view for modifier sales reporting on closed orders. Pre-joins modifier transaction data with modifier list metadata, location, and channel. Reports only on closed (fully settled) orders. Use this view for modifier sales analysis including top-selling modifier sets and most popular modifiers. Modifier list name (modifier_list_name) is the set/group a modifier belongs to (e.g., "Size", "Toppings"), while modifier name (modifier_name) is the specific option chosen (e.g., "Large", "Extra Cheese"). The same modifier_name can appear in different lists (e.g., "Small" in both "Size" and "Portion"), so always group by both modifier_list_name and modifier_name when analyzing individual modifiers. Group by modifier_list_name alone to compare performance across modifier sets.

dimensions.name: OfflinePaymentMethods.reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Offline Payment Methods Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description:
For payments processed by Square, this field will initially be `null`, and will get set
depending on the type of payment method. It will be set based on the following conditions:
- Card and Payments Modeled as Card (Afterpay, PayPay, etc): Set to the record's `authIntent.createdAt`
- Square Gift Cards: Set to the record's `captureIntent.createdAt`
- Bank Transfers: Set to the record's `bankTransaction.completedAt`
- Async Transaction: Set to the record's `asyncTransaction.completedAt`

On failed payments, this field will remain unset permanently

For payments recorded but not processed by Square (which can never fail, and have no
processing lifecycle), this field will always be set, typically to the `createdAt` timestamp

meta.label: Payment timestamp
meta.tooltip: Time when the payment was recorded.

dimensions.name: OfflinePaymentMethods.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Offline Payment Methods Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

cubes.description: Curated pass-through view for offline payments reporting. Exposes offline payment measures together with the most useful grouping and filter dimensions for seller-facing offline payment report surfaces. Includes only payments (not refunds) where the order's payment group has completionProcessingMode set to OFFLINE. Includes FAILED payment status for failure rate analysis. Use this view instead of querying the raw OfflinePayments cube directly.

cubes.name: ProductMixReport

measures.name: ProductMixReport.items_sold_quantity
measures.title: Product Mix Items Sold Quantity
measures.description: Total quantity sold in the item's unit of measure. This may be a count for individually sold items or a measured amount for items sold by weight, volume, time, or another unit. Returns are not included. This matches the Units sold column in the standard Item Sales report.

meta.percentage_tooltip: Share of the total units sold in the current view for this selection.
meta.label: Units sold
meta.tooltip: Total quantity sold in the configured unit (e.g., kg for weight items, count for unit items). Excludes returns. Matches the Units sold column in the standard Item Sales report.
meta.context: Shows quantity sold before returns are deducted. Use Net item quantity when you want sales after returns are subtracted.

measures.name: ProductMixReport.net_sales
measures.title: Product Mix Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales for sold items only

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales amount for sold items only. Does not include returns.
meta.context: Amount of item gross sales minus discounts and comps. Only includes sales, not returns.

measures.name: ProductMixReport.gross_sales
measures.title: Product Mix Gross Sales
measures.description: Gross sales for sold items only

meta.percentage_tooltip: Share of the total gross sales in the current view for this selection.
meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales amount for sold items only. Does not include returns.
meta.context: Amount of money collected in item sales. Only includes sales, not returns.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Product Mix Merchant Id
dimensions.description: The merchant ID

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.location_id
dimensions.title: Product Mix Location Id
dimensions.description: The location ID

meta.label: Location ID
meta.tooltip: Location where the item was sold.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.location_name
dimensions.title: Product Mix Location Name
dimensions.description: Display name/nickname of the location

meta.label: Location name
meta.tooltip: Display name of the location.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.transacted_at
dimensions.title: Product Mix Transacted at
dimensions.description: Transaction timestamp

meta.label: Time of sale
meta.tooltip: Time when payment was received for the item.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Product Mix Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_minute
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_hour
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_date
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_month
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ProductMixReport.local_year
dimensions.title: Product Mix Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.
