cubes.name: Sales

measures.name: Sales.top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Sales Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries - This is the top-line product sales without counting refunds, discounts, comps, refunds, tips, tax, or fees, or sales of gift cards. This is the default measure when 'sales' is referenced ambiguously without specifying net, total, or other qualifiers.

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of money collected in sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, returns, tips, taxes, fees, and gift card sales.

measures.name: Sales.net_sales
measures.title: Sales Net Sales
measures.description: Top line sales after discounts and itemized returns. Equivalent to 'topLineProductSales' - discountsAndComps - itemizedReturns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.
meta.context: Net sales are equal to your gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.

measures.name: Sales.covered_net_sales
measures.title: Sales Covered Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales from orders with one or more covers

meta.label: Covered net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers.
meta.context: Covered net sales only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover.

measures.name: Sales.net_sales_per_cover
measures.title: Sales Net Sales Per Cover
measures.description: Average net sales per cover, excluding orders without covers from the numerator

meta.label: Net sales per cover
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover is calculated as net sales from orders with covers divided by total covers.

measures.name: Sales.total_collected_amount
measures.title: Sales Total Collected Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the customer. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, tax, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding. aka Total Sales

meta.label: Total collected
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected in sales. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, taxes, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding.
meta.context: The total amount you collected in sales. This includes the amount you collected in modifiers, tips, taxes, and gift card sales. It accounts for discounts provided, returns, refunds, and cash rounding as well.

measures.name: Sales.item_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales Item Sales Amount
measures.description: Total item sales including modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales

meta.label: Total item sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount of collected from items sold. Includes modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money collected from items you sold. Modifiers are counted as items. But returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales are excluded.

measures.name: Sales.total_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales Total Sales Amount
measures.description: Total sales amount for the order

meta.label: Total sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales minus discounts/comps/returns, plus taxes/tips/gift cards, minus refunds.
meta.context: Calculated as gross sales (item sales + surcharges) minus discounts, comps, and returns, plus taxes, tips, and gift card sales, then minus refunds. Provides a complete view of all sales activity.

measures.name: Sales.order_count
measures.title: Sales Order Count
measures.description: Total number of orders

meta.label: Number of orders
meta.tooltip: Total number of orders sold.
meta.context: The total number of orders sold.

measures.name: Sales.top_line_product_sales_count
measures.title: Sales Top Line Product Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to top line product sales (gross sales)

meta.label: Gross sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with gross sales.

measures.name: Sales.net_sales_count
measures.title: Sales Net Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to net sales

meta.label: Net sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with net sales.

measures.name: Sales.cover_count
measures.title: Sales Cover Count
measures.description: Number of covers served by the orders

meta.label: Covers
meta.tooltip: Number of covers served.
meta.context: Covers are based on the number of seats on the order's restaurant seating extension.

measures.name: Sales.total_sales_count
measures.title: Sales Total Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to total sales

meta.label: Total sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with total sales.

measures.name: Sales.avg_top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Sales Avg Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Average gross sales per order

meta.label: Average gross sales
meta.tooltip: Average amount of gross sales per order.
meta.context: The average amount of gross sales (top-line product sales) across all of your orders.

measures.name: Sales.avg_net_sales
measures.title: Sales Avg Net Sales
measures.description: Average net sales per order

meta.label: Average net sales
meta.tooltip: Average net sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of net sales (gross sales minus...) you made across all of your orders.

measures.name: Sales.avg_total_collected
measures.title: Sales Avg Total Collected
measures.description: Average total collected amount per order

meta.label: Average total collected
meta.tooltip: Average amount collected in total from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of money you collected across all of your orders.

measures.name: Sales.avg_total_sales
measures.title: Sales Avg Total Sales
measures.description: Average total sales per order

meta.label: Average total sales
meta.tooltip: Average total sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average total sales amount across all of your orders.

measures.name: Sales.discounts_amount
measures.title: Sales Discounts Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary discount deduction, including returns. Discounts applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.label: Discounts
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: Sales.discounts_count
measures.title: Sales Discounts Count
measures.description: Number of orders with discounts applied

meta.label: Discounts order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with discounts.

measures.name: Sales.comps_amount
measures.title: Sales Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

measures.name: Sales.comps_count
measures.title: Sales Comps Count
measures.description: Number of orders with comps applied

meta.label: Comps order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with comps.

measures.name: Sales.itemized_returns
measures.title: Sales Itemized Returns
measures.description: Itemized returns. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales. Also known as GrossSalesReturns

meta.label: Returns
meta.tooltip: Amount of money returned for item sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money returned to customers on sales of items. This excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.

measures.name: Sales.refunds_by_amount_amount
measures.title: Sales Refunds by Amount Amount
measures.description: A non-itemized refund that is purely a money amount given back to the customer. Negative value means less money collected for the sale

meta.label: Refunds
meta.tooltip: Amount of money refunded to a customer for an order.
meta.context: The amount of money refunded to a customer. If this number is negative, it means that you refunded the customer more money than you collected from them for the order.

measures.name: Sales.tips_amount
measures.title: Sales Tips Amount
measures.description: Total of all tips excluding cash tips. Excludes auto gratuity which is a service charge and cash tips since cash tips are not recorded in the Square App

meta.label: Tips (non-cash)
meta.tooltip: Total amount of tips collected. Does not include cash tips or autogratuity.
meta.context: The total amount of tips you collected through Square. This does not include cash tips or autogratuity, which is a service charge.

measures.name: Sales.sales_tax_amount
measures.title: Sales Sales Tax Amount
measures.description: Sales tax amounts applied at the time of sale. Configured by the seller

meta.label: Sales tax
meta.tooltip: Total amount of sales tax applied to an order.
meta.context: The total amount of sales tax applied to an order. This is configured in Square based on your tax jurisdictions.

measures.name: Sales.auto_gratuity_tax_amount
measures.title: Sales Auto Gratuity Tax Amount
measures.description: Tax applied to automatic gratuity charges

meta.label: Service charge tax (Payable to Employees)
meta.tooltip: Tax collected on service charges payable to employees.

measures.name: Sales.gift_card_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales Gift Card Sales Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the sale of Square Gift Cards. Note this metric refers to sales of gift cards, not items purchased using gift cards as a currency. Includes Discounts & Returns. Excludes taxes on gift card sales and surcharges on gift card sales

meta.label: Gift card sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected from the sale of Square gift cards. Includes discounts and returns. Excludes taxes and surcharges.
meta.context: The total amount you collected from the sale of Square gift cards. This does not refer to sales made to customers who pay with a gift card. The amount includes discounts and returns but excludes taxes and surcharges on gift card sales.

measures.name: Sales.location_count
measures.title: Sales Location Count
measures.description: Count of unique locations

meta.label: Locations
meta.tooltip: Total number of locations.

measures.name: Sales.unique_customers
measures.title: Sales Unique Customers
measures.description: Count of unique customers

meta.label: Unique customers
meta.tooltip: Number of unique customers.

measures.name: Sales.customer_visit_count
measures.title: Sales Customer Visit Count
measures.description: Count of customer visits (purchases). An order with multiple customers contributes one visit per customer.

meta.label: Customer visits
meta.tooltip: Total number of customer visits. Each visit represents a customer purchase at the merchant.

measures.name: Sales.team_member_collected_by_count
measures.title: Sales Team Member Collected by Count
measures.description: Count of unique team members

meta.label: Team members
meta.tooltip: Total number of team members.

measures.name: Sales.team_member_attributed_to_count
measures.title: Sales Team Member Attributed to Count
measures.description: Count of unique team members

meta.label: Team members
meta.tooltip: Total number of team members.

dimensions.name: Sales.merchant_id
dimensions.title: Sales Merchant Id
dimensions.description: The merchant ID

dimensions.name: Sales.order_id
dimensions.title: Sales Order Id
dimensions.description: Unique ID for the order

meta.label: Order ID
meta.tooltip: Unique ID associated with the order.

dimensions.name: Sales.order_note

dimensions.name: Sales.invoice_number

dimensions.name: Sales.location_id

meta.tooltip: Location that sold the order.

meta.tooltip: Display name of the location.

dimensions.description: Order Source

meta.label: Order source
meta.tooltip: Source for the order.

dimensions.name: Sales.team_member_collected_by_id
dimensions.title: Sales Team Member Collected by Id
dimensions.description: The single team member token that collected the payment. Set to MULTIPLE_EMPLOYEES if multiple team members collected payments

meta.label: Team member collected by ID
meta.tooltip: Team member who collected payment for the order.
meta.context: The team member who collected payment for the order. If multiple team members collected payment, this will be "multiple employees" instead of a single employee ID.

dimensions.name: Sales.team_member_collected_by_name
dimensions.title: Sales Team Member Collected by Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member who collected payment for the order. Shows 'MULTIPLE_EMPLOYEES' when multiple team members collected payments on the same order

meta.label: Team member collected by
meta.tooltip: Name of the team member who collected payment. When multiple team members collected payments on the same order, this shows MULTIPLE_EMPLOYEES instead of a name.

dimensions.name: Sales.team_member_attributed_to_id

dimensions.description: The team member attributed to the order. Accounts for ownership transfers, ticket assignment, appointment staff, and payment collection

meta.label: Team member attributed to ID
meta.tooltip: The team member attributed to this order for reporting purposes.
meta.context: The team member attributed to this order. This is not necessarily who created or collected the order — it accounts for ownership transfers, ticket assignment, appointment staff, and who collected payment, in that priority order.

dimensions.name: Sales.team_member_attributed_to_name
dimensions.title: Sales Team Member Attributed to Name
dimensions.description: Full name of the team member attributed to the order. Reflects the current name in the team member directory

meta.label: Team member attributed to
meta.tooltip: Name of the team member attributed to this order. Reflects the current name in the team member directory.

cubes.title: Sales

examples.title: Sales by Location
examples.description: What were my sales by location last year?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', Sales.local_reporting_timestamp) AS month,
  MEASURE(Sales.top_line_product_sales) AS gross_sales,
  MEASURE(Sales.order_count) AS order_count
FROM Sales
WHERE Sales.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND Sales.local_reporting_timestamp < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY gross_sales DESC
examples.notes: Use DATE_TRUNC to bucket timestamp. Both top_line_product_sales and count must be wrapped in MEASURE(). Use >= / < date comparisons for filtering — YEAR() and EXTRACT() do not work in Cube SQL. All dimensions must be in GROUP BY

examples.title: Revenue Share by Order Size
examples.description: What percentage of revenue came from large orders (over $50)?
examples.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    Sales.order_id AS order_id,
    MEASURE(Sales.top_line_product_sales) AS amount
  FROM Sales
  WHERE Sales.local_date >= '2025-01-01'
    AND Sales.local_date < '2026-01-01'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN amount > 50 THEN 'Large' ELSE 'Small' END AS order_size,
  SUM(amount) AS total_revenue,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total
FROM base
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
examples.notes: Inner query uses restricted Cube SQL: groups by existing dimensions and uses MEASURE() for aggregations. Outer query uses standard SQL (CASE WHEN, SUM, window functions) for computation

examples.title: Total Discounts
examples.description: How much did discounts cost me this year?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  -MEASURE(Sales.discounts_amount) AS total_discounts,
  MEASURE(Sales.discounts_count) AS discounted_order_count
FROM Sales
WHERE Sales.local_reporting_timestamp >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND Sales.local_reporting_timestamp < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 year'
examples.notes: Sales.discounts_amount is a signed Sales Summary deduction, so sale discounts are negative. For seller-facing total discounts or discount cost questions, negate the measure when the result is negative and present the magnitude as the discount amount. Use discounts_count to sanity-check that discounts were actually applied.

cubes.description: Curated view for sales, orders, and transactions reporting. Reports only on closed (fully settled) orders and includes dimensions for location, channel, customer, and employee analysis. Contains measures for gross/total/net sales and discount, comp, refund, tip amount at the order level. NOTE: Unless "net" or another qualifier is used, default to using top_line measures for questions about sales and order counts -- these represent Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries (see measure descriptions for full details).

cubes.name: SalesByServiceDate

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries - This is the top-line product sales without counting refunds, discounts, comps, refunds, tips, tax, or fees, or sales of gift cards. This is the default measure when 'sales' is referenced ambiguously without specifying net, total, or other qualifiers.

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of money collected in sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, returns, tips, taxes, fees, and gift card sales.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.net_sales
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Net Sales
measures.description: Top line sales after discounts and itemized returns. Equivalent to 'topLineProductSales' - discountsAndComps - itemizedReturns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.
meta.context: Net sales are equal to your gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.covered_net_sales
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Covered Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales from orders with one or more covers

meta.label: Covered net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers.
meta.context: Covered net sales only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.net_sales_per_cover
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Net Sales Per Cover
measures.description: Average net sales per cover, excluding orders without covers from the numerator

meta.label: Net sales per cover
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover is calculated as net sales from orders with covers divided by total covers.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.item_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Item Sales Amount
measures.description: Total item sales including modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales

meta.label: Total item sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount of collected from items sold. Includes modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money collected from items you sold. Modifiers are counted as items. But returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales are excluded.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.total_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Total Sales Amount
measures.description: Total sales amount for the order

meta.label: Total sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales minus discounts/comps/returns, plus taxes/tips/gift cards, minus refunds.
meta.context: Calculated as gross sales (item sales + surcharges) minus discounts, comps, and returns, plus taxes, tips, and gift card sales, then minus refunds. Provides a complete view of all sales activity.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.discounts_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Discounts Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary discount deduction, including returns. Discounts applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.label: Discounts
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.comps_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.itemized_returns
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Itemized Returns
measures.description: Itemized returns. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales. Also known as GrossSalesReturns

meta.label: Returns
meta.tooltip: Amount of money returned for item sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money returned to customers on sales of items. This excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.refunds_by_amount_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Refunds by Amount Amount
measures.description: A non-itemized refund that is purely a money amount given back to the customer. Negative value means less money collected for the sale

meta.label: Refunds
meta.tooltip: Amount of money refunded to a customer for an order.
meta.context: The amount of money refunded to a customer. If this number is negative, it means that you refunded the customer more money than you collected from them for the order.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.tips_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Tips Amount
measures.description: Total of all tips excluding cash tips. Excludes auto gratuity which is a service charge and cash tips since cash tips are not recorded in the Square App

meta.label: Tips (non-cash)
meta.tooltip: Total amount of tips collected. Does not include cash tips or autogratuity.
meta.context: The total amount of tips you collected through Square. This does not include cash tips or autogratuity, which is a service charge.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.sales_tax_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Sales Tax Amount
measures.description: Sales tax amounts applied at the time of sale. Configured by the seller

meta.label: Sales tax
meta.tooltip: Total amount of sales tax applied to an order.
meta.context: The total amount of sales tax applied to an order. This is configured in Square based on your tax jurisdictions.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.auto_gratuity_tax_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Auto Gratuity Tax Amount

meta.label: Service charge tax (Payable to Employees)
meta.tooltip: Tax collected on service charges payable to employees.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDate.gift_card_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date Gift Card Sales Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the sale of Square Gift Cards. Note this metric refers to sales of gift cards, not items purchased using gift cards as a currency. Includes Discounts & Returns. Excludes taxes on gift card sales and surcharges on gift card sales

meta.label: Gift card sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected from the sale of Square gift cards. Includes discounts and returns. Excludes taxes and surcharges.
meta.context: The total amount you collected from the sale of Square gift cards. This does not refer to sales made to customers who pay with a gift card. The amount includes discounts and returns but excludes taxes and surcharges on gift card sales.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date Accrual Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Timestamp used for accrual-based reporting. Uses the invoice service date, the completion time of the last completed fulfillment once all fulfillments are complete, the appointment booking start time when there are no fulfillments, or the time the order became fully paid, in that order. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

meta.label: Service date and time
meta.tooltip: Timestamp used for service-date reporting, based on when services were rendered or, when unavailable, when the order became fully paid.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date Accrual Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_minute
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date Accrual Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_hour
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date Accrual Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_date
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date Accrual Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_day_of_week

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_month

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_year

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDate.accrual_state

dimensions.description: Whether the order has been recognized for accrual-based reporting. Orders whose source accrual state is WILL_BE_RECOGNIZED are exposed here as RECOGNIZED once their service date has passed, and UNRECOGNIZED before then. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

cubes.title: Sales by Service Date

meta.label: Sales by Service Date
meta.tooltip: Shows top-level sales based on when services were rendered or items fulfilled (not when they were fully paid).

catalog.label: Sales by Service Date
catalog.tooltip: Sales, tips, tax, and discounts by service date or fulfillment date

presets.label: Sales by Service Date
presets.tooltip: Sales, tips, tax, and discounts by service date or fulfillment date

examples.title: Sales by Service Date by Location
examples.description: What were my sales by location last year based on when services were rendered?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp) AS month,
  MEASURE(SalesByServiceDate.top_line_product_sales) AS gross_sales,
  MEASURE(SalesByServiceDate.order_count) AS order_count
FROM SalesByServiceDate
WHERE SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY gross_sales DESC
examples.notes: Use DATE_TRUNC to bucket timestamp. Both top_line_product_sales and count must be wrapped in MEASURE(). Use >= / < date comparisons for filtering. This view groups sales by service date instead of when the order was fully paid.

examples.title: Revenue Share by Order Size by Service Date
examples.description: What percentage of revenue came from large orders (over $50) this year based on service date?
examples.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    SalesByServiceDate.order_id AS order_id,
    MEASURE(SalesByServiceDate.top_line_product_sales) AS amount
  FROM SalesByServiceDate
  WHERE SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_date >= '2025-01-01'
    AND SalesByServiceDate.accrual_local_date < '2026-01-01'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN amount > 50 THEN 'Large' ELSE 'Small' END AS order_size,
  SUM(amount) AS total_revenue,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total
FROM base
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
examples.notes: Inner query uses restricted Cube SQL: groups by existing dimensions and uses MEASURE() for aggregations. Outer query uses standard SQL (CASE WHEN, SUM, window functions) for computation. This view uses service date instead of fully paid date.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.discounts_amount

A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.comps_amount
measures.title: Sales by Service Date (UK) Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date (UK) Accrual Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Timestamp used for accrual-based reporting. Uses the invoice service date, the completion time of the last completed fulfillment once all fulfillments are complete, the appointment booking start time when there are no fulfillments, or the time the order became fully paid, in that order. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

meta.label: Service date and time

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date (UK) Accrual Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_minute
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date (UK) Accrual Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_hour
dimensions.title: Sales by Service Date (UK) Accrual Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

dimensions.name: SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_date

dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is still under development and has not been backfilled.

examples.title: UK Sales by Service Date by Location
examples.description: What were my UK sales by location last year based on when services were rendered?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp) AS month,
  MEASURE(SalesByServiceDateUK.top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity) AS product_sales,
  MEASURE(SalesByServiceDateUK.order_count) AS order_count
FROM SalesByServiceDateUK
WHERE SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_reporting_timestamp < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY product_sales DESC
examples.notes: Use DATE_TRUNC to bucket timestamp. Both top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity and count must be wrapped in MEASURE(). Use >= / < date comparisons for filtering. This view groups UK sales by service date instead of when the order was fully paid.

examples.title: UK Revenue Share by Order Size by Service Date
examples.description: What percentage of UK revenue came from large orders (over GBP 50) this year based on service date?
examples.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    SalesByServiceDateUK.order_id AS order_id,
    MEASURE(SalesByServiceDateUK.top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity) AS amount
  FROM SalesByServiceDateUK
  WHERE SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_date >= '2025-01-01'
    AND SalesByServiceDateUK.accrual_local_date < '2026-01-01'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN amount > 50 THEN 'Large' ELSE 'Small' END AS order_size,
  SUM(amount) AS total_revenue,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total
FROM base
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
examples.notes: Inner query uses restricted Cube SQL: groups by existing dimensions and uses MEASURE() for aggregations. Outer query uses standard SQL (CASE WHEN, SUM, window functions) for computation. This UK view uses service date instead of fully paid date.

cubes.name: SalesUK

measures.name: SalesUK.total_collected_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Total Collected Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the customer. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, tax, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding. aka Total Sales

meta.label: Total collected
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected in sales. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, taxes, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding.
meta.context: The total amount you collected in sales. This includes the amount you collected in modifiers, tips, taxes, and gift card sales. It accounts for discounts provided, returns, refunds, and cash rounding as well.

measures.name: SalesUK.total_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Total Sales Amount
measures.description: Total sales amount for the order

meta.label: Total sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales minus discounts/comps/returns, plus taxes/tips/gift cards, minus refunds.
meta.context: Calculated as gross sales (item sales + surcharges) minus discounts, comps, and returns, plus taxes, tips, and gift card sales, then minus refunds. Provides a complete view of all sales activity.

measures.name: SalesUK.top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Top Line Product Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Top Line Product Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Top line product sales excluding automatic gratuity. UK compliance metric. Equivalent to top_line_product_sales minus auto_gratuity_amount

meta.label: Top line product sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Top line product sales excluding service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: SalesUK.item_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Item Sales Amount
measures.description: Total item sales including modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales

meta.label: Total item sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount of collected from items sold. Includes modifiers. Excludes returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money collected from items you sold. Modifiers are counted as items. But returns, refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales are excluded.

measures.name: SalesUK.service_charges_only_sum
measures.title: Sales (UK) Service Charges Only Sum
measures.description: Service charges excluding card surcharges and automatic gratuity. UK sales summary metric.

meta.label: Other service charges
meta.tooltip: Service charges passed on to customers, excluding service charges payable to employees and card surcharges.

measures.name: SalesUK.net_sales_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Net Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net sales excluding automatic gratuity (net = sales - returns). UK compliance metric. Equivalent to net_sales minus net auto_gratuity

meta.label: Net sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Net sales excluding service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: SalesUK.covered_net_sales_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Covered Net Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Covered Net Sales Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net sales excluding automatic gratuity from orders with one or more covers. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Covered net sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers, excluding service charges payable to employees.
meta.context: Covered net sales (UK) only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0 and excludes service charges payable to employees. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover in UK reporting.

measures.name: SalesUK.net_sales_per_cover_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Net Sales Per Cover Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Net Sales Per Cover Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Average net sales per cover excluding automatic gratuity, excluding orders without covers from the numerator. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Net sales per cover (UK)
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales excluding service charges payable to employees divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover (UK) is calculated as net sales from orders with covers, excluding service charges payable to employees, divided by total covers.

measures.name: SalesUK.net_sales_with_tax_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Net Sales with Tax Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.shortTitle: Net Sales with Tax Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net sales with tax, excluding automatic gratuity and its tax (net = sales - returns). "Gross Sales" in UK and similar countries.

meta.label: Gross Sales (UK)
meta.tooltip: Gross sales excluding service charges payable to employees and related tax for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: SalesUK.order_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Order Count
measures.description: Total number of orders

meta.label: Number of orders
meta.tooltip: Total number of orders sold.
meta.context: The total number of orders sold.

measures.name: SalesUK.top_line_product_sales_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Top Line Product Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to top line product sales (gross sales)

meta.label: Gross sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with gross sales.

measures.name: SalesUK.net_sales_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Net Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to net sales

meta.label: Net sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with net sales.

measures.name: SalesUK.cover_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Cover Count
measures.description: Number of covers served by the orders

meta.label: Covers
meta.tooltip: Number of covers served.
meta.context: Covers are based on the number of seats on the order's restaurant seating extension.

measures.name: SalesUK.total_sales_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Total Sales Count
measures.description: Number of orders that contributed to total sales

meta.label: Total sales order count
meta.tooltip: Number of orders with total sales.

measures.name: SalesUK.avg_top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Sales (UK) Avg Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Average gross sales per order

meta.label: Average gross sales
meta.tooltip: Average amount of gross sales per order.
meta.context: The average amount of gross sales (top-line product sales) across all of your orders.

measures.name: SalesUK.avg_net_sales
measures.title: Sales (UK) Avg Net Sales
measures.description: Average net sales per order

meta.label: Average net sales
meta.tooltip: Average net sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of net sales (gross sales minus...) you made across all of your orders.

measures.name: SalesUK.avg_total_collected
measures.title: Sales (UK) Avg Total Collected
measures.description: Average total collected amount per order

meta.label: Average total collected
meta.tooltip: Average amount collected in total from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of money you collected across all of your orders.

measures.name: SalesUK.avg_total_sales
measures.title: Sales (UK) Avg Total Sales
measures.description: Average total sales per order

meta.label: Average total sales
meta.tooltip: Average total sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average total sales amount across all of your orders.

measures.name: SalesUK.discount_and_comps_sum
measures.title: Sales (UK) Discount and Comps Sum
measures.description: Combined signed discounts and comps as shown in Sales Summary. Equivalent to discounts_amount plus comps_amount. Sale discounts and comps are negative deductions because they reduce gross/net sales; negative values are not refunds or reversals by themselves.

meta.label: Discounts & comps
meta.tooltip: Combined discounts and comps applied to the order.

measures.name: SalesUK.discounts_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Discounts Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary discount deduction, including returns. Discounts applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.label: Discounts
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: SalesUK.comps_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

measures.name: SalesUK.returns_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Returns Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Itemized returns excluding returned automatic gratuity. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Returns (UK)
meta.tooltip: Returns excluding service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: SalesUK.refunds_by_amount_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Refunds by Amount Amount
measures.description: A non-itemized refund that is purely a money amount given back to the customer. Negative value means less money collected for the sale

meta.label: Refunds
meta.tooltip: Amount of money refunded to a customer for an order.
meta.context: The amount of money refunded to a customer. If this number is negative, it means that you refunded the customer more money than you collected from them for the order.

measures.name: SalesUK.tips_plus_net_auto_gratuity_sum
measures.title: Sales (UK) Tips Plus Net Auto Gratuity Sum
measures.shortTitle: Tips Plus Net Auto Gratuity Sum
measures.description: Tips plus net automatic gratuity, matching the UK Sales Summary payable-to-employees total.

meta.label: Tips & service charges
meta.tooltip: Total amount payable to employees, including tips and employee-payable service charges.

measures.name: SalesUK.net_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Net Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Net automatic gratuity including tax (net = sales - returns + tax). UK compliance metric for total autogratuity collected.

meta.label: Service charges (Payable to Employees)
meta.tooltip: Net service charges payable to employees, including related tax, for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: SalesUK.tips_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Tips Amount
measures.description: Total of all tips excluding cash tips. Excludes auto gratuity which is a service charge and cash tips since cash tips are not recorded in the Square App

meta.label: Tips (non-cash)
meta.tooltip: Total amount of tips collected. Does not include cash tips or autogratuity.
meta.context: The total amount of tips you collected through Square. This does not include cash tips or autogratuity, which is a service charge.

measures.name: SalesUK.sales_tax_minus_auto_gratuity
measures.title: Sales (UK) Sales Tax Minus Auto Gratuity
measures.description: Sales tax excluding tax on automatic gratuity. UK compliance metric.

meta.label: Sales tax (UK)
meta.tooltip: Sales tax excluding tax on service charges payable to employees for UK reporting compliance.

measures.name: SalesUK.auto_gratuity_tax_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Auto Gratuity Tax Amount
measures.description: Tax applied to automatic gratuity charges

meta.label: Service charge tax (Payable to Employees)
meta.tooltip: Tax collected on service charges payable to employees.

measures.name: SalesUK.gift_card_sales_amount
measures.title: Sales (UK) Gift Card Sales Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the sale of Square Gift Cards. Note this metric refers to sales of gift cards, not items purchased using gift cards as a currency. Includes Discounts & Returns. Excludes taxes on gift card sales and surcharges on gift card sales

meta.label: Gift card sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected from the sale of Square gift cards. Includes discounts and returns. Excludes taxes and surcharges.
meta.context: The total amount you collected from the sale of Square gift cards. This does not refer to sales made to customers who pay with a gift card. The amount includes discounts and returns but excludes taxes and surcharges on gift card sales.

measures.name: SalesUK.location_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Location Count
measures.description: Count of unique locations

meta.label: Locations
meta.tooltip: Total number of locations.

measures.name: SalesUK.unique_customers
measures.title: Sales (UK) Unique Customers

meta.label: Unique customers
meta.tooltip: Number of unique customers.

measures.name: SalesUK.customer_visit_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Customer Visit Count
measures.description: Count of customer visits (purchases). An order with multiple customers contributes one visit per customer.

meta.label: Customer visits
meta.tooltip: Total number of customer visits. Each visit represents a customer purchase at the merchant.

measures.name: SalesUK.team_member_attributed_to_count
measures.title: Sales (UK) Team Member Attributed to Count

meta.label: Team members
meta.tooltip: Total number of team members.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_minute
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_hour
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_date
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_month
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SalesUK.local_year
dimensions.title: Sales (UK) Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

cubes.title: Sales (UK)

meta.label: Sales UK
meta.tooltip: Curated view for UK sales reporting with GB-specific sales metrics that exclude automatic gratuity where applicable.

catalog.label: Sales UK
catalog.tooltip: Sales, tips, tax, and discounts with UK-specific sales metrics

presets.label: Sales UK
presets.tooltip: Sales, tips, tax, and discounts with UK-specific sales metrics

examples.title: UK Sales by Location
examples.description: What were my UK sales by location last year?
examples.sql_query:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', SalesUK.local_reporting_timestamp) AS month,
  MEASURE(SalesUK.top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity) AS product_sales,
  MEASURE(SalesUK.order_count) AS order_count
FROM SalesUK
WHERE SalesUK.local_reporting_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
  AND SalesUK.local_reporting_timestamp < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY product_sales DESC
examples.notes: Use DATE_TRUNC to bucket timestamp. Both top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity and count must be wrapped in MEASURE(). Use >= / < date comparisons for filtering - YEAR() and EXTRACT() do not work in Cube SQL. All dimensions must be in GROUP BY

examples.title: UK Revenue Share by Order Size
examples.description: What percentage of UK revenue came from large orders (over GBP 50)?
examples.sql_query:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    SalesUK.order_id AS order_id,
    MEASURE(SalesUK.top_line_product_sales_minus_auto_gratuity) AS amount
  FROM SalesUK
  WHERE SalesUK.local_date >= '2025-01-01'
    AND SalesUK.local_date < '2026-01-01'
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN amount > 50 THEN 'Large' ELSE 'Small' END AS order_size,
  SUM(amount) AS total_revenue,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) / SUM(SUM(amount)) OVER (), 2) AS pct_of_total
FROM base
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
examples.notes: Inner query uses restricted Cube SQL: groups by existing dimensions and uses MEASURE() for aggregations. Outer query uses standard SQL (CASE WHEN, SUM, window functions) for computation

cubes.description: Curated view for UK sales, orders, and transactions reporting. Reports only on closed (fully settled) orders and includes dimensions for location, channel, customer, and employee analysis. Use this view instead of Sales for UK and GB reporting where Auto Gratuity Service Charges are not counted as revenue, as required in UK markets. Mirrors the Sales view, but adds UK-specific measures that exclude automatic gratuity from revenue metrics so merchants can use the same locale-specific metrics shown in Sales Summary.

cubes.name: SellThrough

measures.name: SellThrough.current_quantity
measures.title: Sell Through Current Quantity
measures.description: Current IN_STOCK quantity as of now

measures.name: SellThrough.end_of_period_quantity
measures.title: Sell Through End of Period Quantity
measures.description: IN_STOCK quantity at the end of the selected reporting period, reconstructed by subtracting stock changes after the period end from the current quantity

measures.name: SellThrough.on_order_quantity
measures.title: Sell Through On Order Quantity
measures.description: Current quantity on order from vendor (ORDERED_FROM_VENDOR state) as of now

measures.name: SellThrough.in_transit_quantity
measures.title: Sell Through In Transit Quantity
measures.description: Current quantity in transit to this location (IN_TRANSIT state) as of now

measures.name: SellThrough.net_stock_change
measures.title: Sell Through Net Stock Change
measures.description: Net stock change within the reporting period. Positive means stock increased, negative means decreased

measures.name: SellThrough.units_received
measures.title: Sell Through Units Received
measures.description: Net units added into available stock during the reporting period. This includes adjustments into IN_STOCK, minus units sent to transit or moved out to another location. Internal movements cancel out when aggregating across locations

measures.name: SellThrough.starting_count
measures.title: Sell Through Starting Count
measures.description: Starting IN_STOCK quantity at the beginning of the reporting period, computed as end-of-period IN_STOCK minus net stock changes during the reporting period. Uses the same reverse-replay logic as InventoryHistoricalCount

measures.name: SellThrough.units_sold
measures.title: Sell Through Units Sold
measures.description: Units sold from closed orders during the reporting period. Computed from line item quantities in reportable, closed-check orders

measures.name: SellThrough.net_sales
measures.title: Sell Through Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales amount for sold items during the reporting period. Computed from closed, reportable order line items after discounts and comps, excluding tax and service charges, and excludes returns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales amount for sold items only. Does not include returns.
meta.context: Amount of item sales after discounts and comps during the reporting period. Excludes tax, service charges, and returns.

measures.name: SellThrough.gross_sales
measures.title: Sell Through Gross Sales
measures.description: Gross sales amount for sold items during the reporting period. Computed from closed, reportable order line items and excludes returns

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales amount for sold items only. Does not include returns.
meta.context: Amount of money collected in item sales during the reporting period. Only includes sales, not returns.

measures.name: SellThrough.sales_velocity
measures.title: Sell Through Sales Velocity
measures.description: Average units sold during the reporting period, normalized to a daily, weekly, or monthly cadence based on reporting window length

meta.label: Sales velocity
meta.tooltip: Average units sold during the reporting period, normalized to a display cadence based on the reporting window.
meta.context: Average units sold during the reporting period. Uses per day for windows under 10 days, per week for windows under 3 months, and per month for longer windows.

measures.name: SellThrough.sku_measure
measures.title: Sell Through Sku Measure
measures.description: Stock Keeping Unit for the item variation. Returns MULTIPLE_SKUS when the aggregation contains more than one SKU

measures.name: SellThrough.gtin_measure
measures.title: Sell Through Gtin Measure
measures.description: GTIN for the item variation. Returns MULTIPLE_GTINS when the aggregation contains more than one GTIN

measures.name: SellThrough.category_measure
measures.title: Sell Through Category Measure
measures.description: Reporting category for the item. Returns MULTIPLE_CATEGORIES when the aggregation contains more than one category

measures.name: SellThrough.last_sold_at_measure
measures.title: Sell Through Last Sold at Measure
measures.description: Most recent date when any item variation in the aggregation was sold during the reporting period

measures.name: SellThrough.last_received_at_measure
measures.title: Sell Through Last Received at Measure
measures.description: Most recent date when any item variation in the aggregation received inventory during the reporting period

measures.name: SellThrough.sell_through_rate
measures.title: Sell Through Sell Through Rate
measures.description: Sell-through rate: Units Sold / (Starting Count + Units Received). Measures what fraction of available inventory was sold. Higher values indicate faster-moving inventory

meta.label: Sell-through rate
meta.tooltip: How much of your stock was sold in the period. Higher means faster-moving inventory.
meta.context: How much of your stock was sold in the period. Calculated as units sold divided by available inventory (starting count plus units received). Higher means faster-moving inventory.

measures.name: SellThrough.days_of_supply
measures.title: Sell Through Days of Supply
measures.description: Estimated days of supply remaining at the end of the reporting period, using the daily sales rate from the selected reporting period. NULL when there are no sales in the period

meta.label: Days of supply
meta.tooltip: Estimated days until stock runs out at the current sales pace.
meta.context: Estimated days until stock runs out at the current sales pace. Calculated from current inventory divided by your average daily sales (units sold divided by days in the period).

measures.name: SellThrough.estimated_sold_out_at
measures.title: Sell Through Estimated Sold out at
measures.description: Estimated date when stock will run out, calculated as the end of the reporting period plus days of supply, capped at 2 years out. NULL when there are no sales in the period

meta.label: Estimated sold out date
meta.tooltip: Estimated date when stock will run out at the current sales pace.
meta.context: Estimated sold out date based on ending on-hand quantity and sales velocity. Calculated as the end of the reporting period plus days of supply.

dimensions.description: Filter this dimension to set the analysis window. All inventory changes, units received, and units sold are computed for the filtered period. For example, setting to 'last 30 days' computes sell-through and turnover for the past 30 days. Do not use this dimension for grouping

dimensions.name: SellThrough.post_reporting_period
dimensions.title: Sell Through Post Reporting Period
dimensions.description: Filter this dimension with an open-ended lower bound beginning immediately after the reporting period. This reconstructs end-of-period quantity from later stock changes. For example, if reporting_period ends on 2026-04-15, filter post_reporting_period after or on 2026-04-16. Do not use this dimension for grouping

cubes.description: Curated pass-through view for sell-through reporting. Exposes the InventoryMetrics reporting measures together with friendly location and item variation fields for seller-facing reports. Historical reports should filter both reporting_period and post_reporting_period, where post_reporting_period starts immediately after the reporting period. Use this view for sell-through report surfaces instead of querying the raw InventoryMetrics cube directly.

cubes.name: ServiceChargesReport

measures.name: ServiceChargesReport.total_service_charge_amount
measures.title: Service Charges Total Service Charge Amount
measures.description: Net service charge amount collected across all orders (sales positive, returns negative). Includes custom service charges, card surcharges, and automatic gratuity. Does not include taxes on the service charges.

meta.label: Total service charges
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected in service charges from all orders.
meta.context: This is the total amount collected in service charges including custom fees, card surcharges, and automatic gratuity. Tax on service charges is tracked separately.

measures.name: ServiceChargesReport.total_service_charge_tax
measures.title: Service Charges Total Service Charge Tax
measures.description: Net tax amount collected on service charges across all orders (sales positive, returns negative)

meta.label: Service charge tax

meta.context: This is the tax collected specifically on service charges, not on the items themselves.

measures.name: ServiceChargesReport.total_with_tax
measures.title: Service Charges Total with Tax
measures.description: Net service charge amount plus tax (sales positive, returns negative). This is the total amount collected from customers for service charges including tax

meta.label: Total service charges with tax
meta.tooltip: Total service charge amount including tax.

measures.name: ServiceChargesReport.order_count
measures.title: Service Charges Order Count

meta.label: Number of service charges
meta.tooltip: Total number of service charges applied.

measures.name: ServiceChargesReport.avg_service_charge_amount

meta.tooltip: Average amount of service charges.

measures.name: ServiceChargesReport.avg_service_charge_per_order

meta.tooltip: Average service charge amount per order.

dimensions.description: The name of the service charge as set by the merchant. NULL for AUTO_GRATUITY type service charges

meta.label: Service charge name
meta.tooltip: Name of the service charge.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.service_charge_type
dimensions.title: Service Charges Service Charge Type
dimensions.description: The type of service charge: CUSTOM (merchant-defined fee), CARD_SURCHARGE (card processing fee), or AUTO_GRATUITY (automatic tip for large parties)

meta.label: Service charge type
meta.tooltip: Type of service charge applied.
meta.context: The type of service charge. This can be Custom (merchant-defined fee), Card Surcharge (card processing fee), or Auto Gratuity (automatic tip).

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.transaction_type
dimensions.title: Service Charges Transaction Type
dimensions.description: Type of transaction: SALE for service charges on sales, RETURN for service charges on returns

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.currency
dimensions.title: Service Charges Currency
dimensions.description: Currency code for the service charge amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP)

meta.label: Currency
meta.tooltip: Currency used for the service charge.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Service Charges Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_date
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Day of Week
dimensions.shortTitle: Local Revenue Recognition Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_month
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_year
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_hour
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: ServiceChargesReport.local_revenue_recognition_minute
dimensions.title: Service Charges Local Revenue Recognition Minute
dimensions.description: Minute of hour (0-59) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for minute-level filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.avg_order_entry_time_ms

meta.label: Avg order entry time

measures.name: SpeedOfService.avg_service_time_ms
measures.title: Speed of Service Avg Service Time Ms
measures.description: Average total time of service, from order entry to payment, in milliseconds

meta.label: Avg service time
meta.tooltip: Average time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.max_order_entry_time_ms
measures.title: Speed of Service Max Order Entry Time Ms
measures.description: Maximum order entry time in milliseconds

meta.label: Max order entry time
meta.tooltip: Longest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the first item is sent to the kitchen.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.max_service_time_ms
measures.title: Speed of Service Max Service Time Ms
measures.description: Maximum service time in milliseconds

meta.label: Max service time
meta.tooltip: Longest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.min_order_entry_time_ms
measures.title: Speed of Service Min Order Entry Time Ms
measures.description: Minimum order entry time in milliseconds

meta.label: Min order entry time
meta.tooltip: Shortest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the first item is sent to the kitchen.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.min_service_time_ms
measures.title: Speed of Service Min Service Time Ms
