meta.label: Min service time
meta.tooltip: Shortest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.order_count
measures.title: Speed of Service Order Count
measures.description: Total number of orders

meta.label: Number of orders
meta.tooltip: Total number of orders sold.
meta.context: The total number of orders sold.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Speed of Service Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries - This is the top-line product sales without counting refunds, discounts, comps, refunds, tips, tax, or fees, or sales of gift cards. This is the default measure when 'sales' is referenced ambiguously without specifying net, total, or other qualifiers.

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of money collected in sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, returns, tips, taxes, fees, and gift card sales.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.net_sales
measures.title: Speed of Service Net Sales
measures.description: Top line sales after discounts and itemized returns. Equivalent to 'topLineProductSales' - discountsAndComps - itemizedReturns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.
meta.context: Net sales are equal to your gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.avg_net_sales
measures.title: Speed of Service Avg Net Sales
measures.description: Average net sales per order

meta.label: Average net sales
meta.tooltip: Average net sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of net sales (gross sales minus...) you made across all of your orders.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.total_collected_amount
measures.title: Speed of Service Total Collected Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the customer. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, tax, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding. aka Total Sales

meta.label: Total collected
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected in sales. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, taxes, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding.
meta.context: The total amount you collected in sales. This includes the amount you collected in modifiers, tips, taxes, and gift card sales. It accounts for discounts provided, returns, refunds, and cash rounding as well.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.tips_amount
measures.title: Speed of Service Tips Amount
measures.description: Total of all tips excluding cash tips. Excludes auto gratuity which is a service charge and cash tips since cash tips are not recorded in the Square App

meta.label: Tips (non-cash)
meta.tooltip: Total amount of tips collected. Does not include cash tips or autogratuity.
meta.context: The total amount of tips you collected through Square. This does not include cash tips or autogratuity, which is a service charge.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.discounts_amount
measures.title: Speed of Service Discounts Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary discount deduction, including returns. Discounts applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.label: Discounts
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.comps_amount
measures.title: Speed of Service Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.itemized_returns
measures.title: Speed of Service Itemized Returns
measures.description: Itemized returns. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales. Also known as GrossSalesReturns

meta.label: Returns
meta.tooltip: Amount of money returned for item sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money returned to customers on sales of items. This excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.sales_tax_amount
measures.title: Speed of Service Sales Tax Amount
measures.description: Sales tax amounts applied at the time of sale. Configured by the seller

meta.label: Sales tax
meta.tooltip: Total amount of sales tax applied to an order.
meta.context: The total amount of sales tax applied to an order. This is configured in Square based on your tax jurisdictions.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.team_member_attributed_to_count
measures.title: Speed of Service Team Member Attributed to Count
measures.description: Count of unique team members

meta.label: Team members
meta.tooltip: Total number of team members.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.kds_ticket_count
measures.title: Speed of Service Kds Ticket Count
measures.description: Count of kitchen tickets (chits per station). Each ticket represents one station's view of a kitchen order. The same order may produce multiple tickets if it is routed to multiple stations (e.g., Prep then Expo). Exposed as 'ticket_count' on the KDS view and 'kds_ticket_count' on the SpeedOfService view

meta.label: Kitchen tickets
meta.tooltip: Total number of kitchen tickets (one per chit per station).

measures.name: SpeedOfService.total_line_items
measures.title: Speed of Service Total Line Items
measures.description: Total item quantity across all tickets

meta.label: Total items
meta.tooltip: Total item quantity across all tickets.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.avg_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Speed of Service Avg Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Average (mean) time in seconds from when a ticket appeared on a station's KDS screen to when the last item on that ticket was marked complete. WARNING: heavily skewed by outlier tickets that were left open for hours (e.g., never marked done at end of shift). For 'typical' or representative completion time, prefer median_ticket_time_seconds. Group by station_type to compare Prep vs. Expo performance, or join to DeviceCodes and group by DeviceCodes.device_code_name to compare individual stations

meta.label: Avg ticket time
meta.tooltip: Average seconds from ticket display on the KDS screen to completion of the last item. Skewed by outliers — prefer median for typical completion time.
meta.context: Measures how long a station takes to complete all items on a ticket. The clock starts when the ticket first appears on the station's screen (display_on_kds_at) and stops when the last line item is marked done (actual_completed_at). The mean is sensitive to outlier tickets — tickets occasionally remain open for hours and inflate the mean by orders of magnitude. For seller-facing 'typical time' questions, prefer median_ticket_time_seconds, which is robust to outliers without imposing an arbitrary cutoff. To identify slow stations, group this measure by DeviceCodes.device_code_name or station_type.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.min_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Speed of Service Min Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Minimum actual time in seconds from ticket display to completion

meta.label: Min prep time
meta.tooltip: Minimum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.max_ticket_time_seconds
measures.title: Speed of Service Max Ticket Time Seconds
measures.description: Maximum actual time in seconds from ticket display to completion

meta.label: Max prep time
meta.tooltip: Maximum time in seconds from display to completion.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.tickets_completed_on_time

meta.label: On-time tickets
meta.tooltip: Number of tickets completed on or before their target time.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.tickets_completed_late
measures.title: Speed of Service Tickets Completed Late
measures.description: Count of tickets with a time due that were completed after that time

meta.label: Late tickets
meta.tooltip: Number of tickets completed after their target time.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.tickets_with_time_due
measures.title: Speed of Service Tickets with Time Due
measures.description: Count of tickets that have a time due

meta.label: Tickets with target time
meta.tooltip: Number of tickets with a target completion time.

measures.name: SpeedOfService.percent_late

measures.description: Percentage of tickets (with a time due) that were completed late

meta.label: Percent late
meta.tooltip: Percentage of tickets completed after their target time.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.sale_timestamp

dimensions.description: Preferred timestamp for sales reporting, roughly based on cash accounting and set to the time the final payments were received. Use this, or the local_reporting_timestamp/local_* dimensions derived from it, for sales, transactions, revenue, tips, discounts, busiest sales hours, and year-over-year sales comparisons unless the user explicitly asks when orders were created or placed.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.created_at
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Created at
dimensions.description: The timestamp when the order was first created or placed. Use this only for questions about order creation/placement timing. For sales, transactions, revenue, reporting periods, busiest sales hours, and year-over-year sales comparisons, prefer sale_timestamp/local_reporting_timestamp or the local_* dimensions derived from it.

meta.label: Order creation time
meta.tooltip: Time that the order was created.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.local_date
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.local_hour
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.local_month

This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: SpeedOfService.station_type
dimensions.title: Speed of Service Station Type
dimensions.description: The role of the kitchen station that processed this ticket. `kds` stations are prep/make-line stations where food and drinks are prepared (e.g., a 'Grill' station or 'Hot Drinks' station). `kds_expo` stations are expeditor stations where completed items are reviewed and assembled before being sent to the customer. To see the specific station name (e.g., 'Hot Drinks'), join to DeviceCodes and use DeviceCodes.device_code_name

meta.label: Station type
meta.tooltip: The role of the kitchen station: Prep (where items are made) or Expo (where orders are assembled and reviewed before serving).
meta.context: Stations are the individual KDS screens in a kitchen, each assigned a role. `kds`: A preparation/make-line station where kitchen staff make specific items (e.g., a grill station, a drinks station). `kds_expo`: An expeditor station that reviews and assembles completed items from prep stations before they go to the customer. A single kitchen can have many `kds` (prep) stations but typically one or two `kds_expo` stations. To compare performance between individual stations (not just station types), join to the DeviceCodes cube and group by DeviceCodes.device_code_name.

catalog.label: Speed of Service
catalog.tooltip: Order entry and completion times

presets.label: Speed of Service
presets.tooltip: Order entry and completion times

cubes.description: Curated view for speed of service and sales reporting. Uses order data from Databricks, pre-filtered to closed checks only (fully paid orders excluding house account invoices). Includes speed of service metrics (order entry time, service time) alongside standard sales measures and optional Kitchen Display System (KDS) ticket metrics. Supports grouping by location, channel, fulfillment type, and team member.

cubes.name: TableTurn

measures.name: TableTurn.median_turn_time_ms
measures.title: Table Turn Median Turn Time Ms
measures.description: Median time from order entry to payment, in milliseconds. Only positive durations are counted — invalid non-positive values (e.g. payment timestamp before order entry from clock skew) are excluded. More robust than the average turn time, which is skewed by checks left open for hours. Turn time is only recorded when speed-of-service data is captured, so it is unavailable for turns without it

meta.label: Median turn time
meta.tooltip: Median time between the first item added and payment. Robust to checks left open for hours.
meta.context: Median turn time is the time from when the first item is added to an order until it is paid. Prefer it over average turn time, which is heavily skewed by orders left open for hours. Turn time is only populated when speed-of-service is captured for the order.

measures.name: TableTurn.avg_service_time_ms
measures.title: Table Turn Avg Service Time Ms
measures.description: Average total time of service, from order entry to payment, in milliseconds

meta.label: Avg service time
meta.tooltip: Average time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: TableTurn.max_service_time_ms
measures.title: Table Turn Max Service Time Ms
measures.description: Maximum service time in milliseconds

meta.label: Max service time
meta.tooltip: Longest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: TableTurn.min_service_time_ms
measures.title: Table Turn Min Service Time Ms
measures.description: Minimum service time in milliseconds

meta.label: Min service time
meta.tooltip: Shortest time between when the first item is added to an order and when the order is paid for.

measures.name: TableTurn.avg_order_entry_time_ms
measures.title: Table Turn Avg Order Entry Time Ms
measures.description: Average time to fully enter an order, from entry start to first item send, in milliseconds

meta.label: Avg order entry time
meta.tooltip: Average time between when the first item is added to an order and when the first item is sent to the kitchen.

measures.name: TableTurn.turn_count
measures.title: Table Turn Turn Count
measures.description: Total number of orders

meta.label: Number of orders
meta.tooltip: Total number of orders sold.
meta.context: The total number of orders sold.

measures.name: TableTurn.cover_count
measures.title: Table Turn Cover Count
measures.description: Number of covers served by the orders

meta.label: Covers
meta.tooltip: Number of covers served.
meta.context: Covers are based on the number of seats on the order's restaurant seating extension.

measures.name: TableTurn.covered_turn_count
measures.title: Table Turn Covered Turn Count
measures.description: Number of table turns with a recorded cover count greater than zero

meta.label: Turns with covers
meta.tooltip: Number of turns with at least one cover recorded.
meta.context: Turns with covers counts only the turns where the seller recorded a cover count. Use it as the denominator for covers per turn and to gauge how complete cover tracking is.

measures.name: TableTurn.covers_per_turn
measures.title: Table Turn Covers Per Turn
measures.description: Average number of covers per table turn, excluding turns without a recorded cover count from the denominator

meta.label: Covers per turn
meta.tooltip: Covers served divided by the number of turns with covers recorded.
meta.context: Covers per turn is total covers served divided by the number of turns that have a recorded cover count. Use it to gauge average party size per seating. Turns without cover data are left out of the calculation so sparse cover tracking does not drag the average down.

measures.name: TableTurn.distinct_tables
measures.title: Table Turn Distinct Tables
measures.description: Number of distinct dining tables that had at least one turn. Scoped by location so identically-named tables at different locations (e.g. 'Patio 2') are counted separately

meta.label: Tables used
meta.tooltip: Number of distinct dining tables with at least one turn.

measures.name: TableTurn.table_days
measures.title: Table Turn Table Days
measures.description: Number of distinct table-day combinations with at least one turn, scoped by location. A table seated on three different days counts as three table-days

meta.label: Table-days in use
meta.tooltip: Number of table and day combinations with at least one turn.
meta.context: Table-days in use counts each day a table had at least one turn. A table seated on three different days counts as three table-days. It is the denominator for turns per table, so multi-day date ranges average per day instead of adding up.

measures.name: TableTurn.turns_per_table
measures.title: Table Turn Turns Per Table
measures.description: Average number of turns per table for each day the table was in use. Uses a table-day denominator so the ratio stays comparable across single-day and multi-day date ranges

meta.label: Turns per table
meta.tooltip: Average turns per table for each day it was in use.
meta.context: Turns per table divides turns by table-days in use, so it reads as the average number of times a table was seated per day it saw service. A higher value means tables were re-seated more often. The denominator only counts days a table was actually used, so days a table sat empty do not lower the average.

measures.name: TableTurn.covered_net_sales
measures.title: Table Turn Covered Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales from orders with one or more covers

meta.label: Covered net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers.
meta.context: Covered net sales only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover.

measures.name: TableTurn.net_sales
measures.title: Table Turn Net Sales
measures.description: Top line sales after discounts and itemized returns. Equivalent to 'topLineProductSales' - discountsAndComps - itemizedReturns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.
meta.context: Net sales are equal to your gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.

measures.name: TableTurn.net_sales_per_cover
measures.title: Table Turn Net Sales Per Cover
measures.description: Average net sales per cover, excluding orders without covers from the numerator

meta.label: Net sales per cover
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover is calculated as net sales from orders with covers divided by total covers.

measures.name: TableTurn.top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Table Turn Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries - This is the top-line product sales without counting refunds, discounts, comps, refunds, tips, tax, or fees, or sales of gift cards. This is the default measure when 'sales' is referenced ambiguously without specifying net, total, or other qualifiers.

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of money collected in sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, returns, tips, taxes, fees, and gift card sales.

cubes.name: TeamSales

measures.name: TeamSales.order_count
measures.title: Team Sales Order Count
measures.description: Total number of orders

meta.label: Number of orders
meta.tooltip: Total number of orders sold.
meta.context: The total number of orders sold.

measures.name: TeamSales.top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Team Sales Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Gross Sales in US and similar countries, or Product Sales in AU and similar countries - This is the top-line product sales without counting refunds, discounts, comps, refunds, tips, tax, or fees, or sales of gift cards. This is the default measure when 'sales' is referenced ambiguously without specifying net, total, or other qualifiers.

meta.label: Gross sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of money collected in sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, returns, tips, taxes, fees, and gift card sales.

measures.name: TeamSales.net_sales
measures.title: Team Sales Net Sales
measures.description: Top line sales after discounts and itemized returns. Equivalent to 'topLineProductSales' - discountsAndComps - itemizedReturns

meta.label: Net sales
meta.tooltip: Amount of gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.
meta.context: Net sales are equal to your gross sales minus discounts, comps, returns, refunds, tips, taxes, fees, or gift card sales.

measures.name: TeamSales.covered_net_sales
measures.title: Team Sales Covered Net Sales
measures.description: Net sales from orders with one or more covers

meta.label: Covered net sales
meta.tooltip: Net sales from orders with covers.
meta.context: Covered net sales only includes orders where cover count is greater than 0. Use it as the numerator for net sales per cover.

measures.name: TeamSales.net_sales_per_cover
measures.title: Team Sales Net Sales Per Cover
measures.description: Average net sales per cover, excluding orders without covers from the numerator

meta.label: Net sales per cover
meta.tooltip: Covered net sales divided by number of covers served.
meta.context: Net sales per cover is calculated as net sales from orders with covers divided by total covers.

measures.name: TeamSales.total_collected_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Total Collected Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the customer. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, tax, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding. aka Total Sales

meta.label: Total collected
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected in sales. Includes modifiers, discounts, tips, taxes, gift card sales, returns, refunds, and cash rounding.
meta.context: The total amount you collected in sales. This includes the amount you collected in modifiers, tips, taxes, and gift card sales. It accounts for discounts provided, returns, refunds, and cash rounding as well.

measures.name: TeamSales.total_sales_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Total Sales Amount
measures.description: Total sales amount for the order

meta.label: Total sales
meta.tooltip: Gross sales minus discounts/comps/returns, plus taxes/tips/gift cards, minus refunds.
meta.context: Calculated as gross sales (item sales + surcharges) minus discounts, comps, and returns, plus taxes, tips, and gift card sales, then minus refunds. Provides a complete view of all sales activity.

measures.name: TeamSales.tips_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Tips Amount
measures.description: Total of all tips excluding cash tips. Excludes auto gratuity which is a service charge and cash tips since cash tips are not recorded in the Square App

meta.label: Tips (non-cash)
meta.tooltip: Total amount of tips collected. Does not include cash tips or autogratuity.
meta.context: The total amount of tips you collected through Square. This does not include cash tips or autogratuity, which is a service charge.

measures.name: TeamSales.comps_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Comps Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary comp deduction, including returns. Comps applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total comps' or 'how much lost on comps,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(comps_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

meta.label: Comps
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in comps applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary comp deduction applied to orders. Negative sale comp amounts are real comps that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the amount comped. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and discounts.

measures.name: TeamSales.discounts_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Discounts Amount
measures.description: Signed Sales Summary discount deduction, including returns. Discounts applied to sales are negative because they reduce gross/net sales. A negative value is not a refund or reversal by itself. For seller-facing 'total discounts' or 'how much lost on discounts,' report the magnitude, e.g. -MEASURE(discounts_amount) when the result is negative. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

meta.label: Discounts
meta.tooltip: Total amount of money in discounts applied to an order. Includes returns. Excludes gift card sales, taxes, and combos.
meta.context: The signed Sales Summary discount deduction applied to orders. Negative sale discount amounts are real discounts that reduced sales, not refunds or reversals by themselves. For seller-facing totals, present the magnitude of negative values as the discount amount. This excludes gift card sales, taxes, and comps.

measures.name: TeamSales.itemized_returns
measures.title: Team Sales Itemized Returns
measures.description: Itemized returns. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, or gift card sales. Also known as GrossSalesReturns

meta.label: Returns
meta.tooltip: Amount of money returned for item sales. Excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.
meta.context: The total amount of money returned to customers on sales of items. This excludes refunds, discounts, comps, taxes, tips, and gift card sales.

measures.name: TeamSales.sales_tax_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Sales Tax Amount
measures.description: Sales tax amounts applied at the time of sale. Configured by the seller

meta.label: Sales tax
meta.tooltip: Total amount of sales tax applied to an order.
meta.context: The total amount of sales tax applied to an order. This is configured in Square based on your tax jurisdictions.

measures.name: TeamSales.gift_card_sales_amount
measures.title: Team Sales Gift Card Sales Amount
measures.description: Total amount collected from the sale of Square Gift Cards. Note this metric refers to sales of gift cards, not items purchased using gift cards as a currency. Includes Discounts & Returns. Excludes taxes on gift card sales and surcharges on gift card sales

meta.label: Gift card sales
meta.tooltip: Total amount collected from the sale of Square gift cards. Includes discounts and returns. Excludes taxes and surcharges.
meta.context: The total amount you collected from the sale of Square gift cards. This does not refer to sales made to customers who pay with a gift card. The amount includes discounts and returns but excludes taxes and surcharges on gift card sales.

measures.name: TeamSales.cover_count
measures.title: Team Sales Cover Count
measures.description: Number of covers served by the orders

meta.label: Covers
meta.tooltip: Number of covers served.
meta.context: Covers are based on the number of seats on the order's restaurant seating extension.

measures.name: TeamSales.avg_net_sales
measures.title: Team Sales Avg Net Sales
measures.description: Average net sales per order

meta.label: Average net sales
meta.tooltip: Average net sales from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of net sales (gross sales minus...) you made across all of your orders.

measures.name: TeamSales.avg_total_collected
measures.title: Team Sales Avg Total Collected
measures.description: Average total collected amount per order

meta.label: Average total collected
meta.tooltip: Average amount collected in total from all orders.
meta.context: The average amount of money you collected across all of your orders.

measures.name: TeamSales.avg_top_line_product_sales
measures.title: Team Sales Avg Top Line Product Sales
measures.description: Average gross sales per order

meta.label: Average gross sales
meta.tooltip: Average amount of gross sales per order.
meta.context: The average amount of gross sales (top-line product sales) across all of your orders.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Team Sales Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_date
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_month
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_year
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: TeamSales.local_hour
dimensions.title: Team Sales Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.sku
dimensions.title: Voids Sku

meta.label: SKU
meta.tooltip: SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) of the item voided

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.check_state
dimensions.title: Voids Check State
dimensions.description: Check state of the order indicating payment and settlement status. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled (standard state for Sales Summary reporting). AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed - this includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. IMPORTANT: For standard sales reporting, filter using the 'closed_checks' segment to match Sales Summary report behavior.

meta.label: Check state
meta.tooltip: The check state of the order: open (not yet paid), awaiting capture (completed but not settled), or closed (paid and settled). Use the closed_checks segment for standard reporting.
meta.context: Check state indicates where an order is in the payment and settlement lifecycle. CLOSED_CHECK: Orders that have been fully paid and settled. This is the standard state for completed sales and matches Sales Summary reporting. Also includes unpaid house account orders. AWAITING_CAPTURE_CHECK: Orders that have received payment but are not yet fully closed. This includes orders with paper tips that have not been completed yet and re-openable orders that have not been closed out. These orders are in a temporary state awaiting final settlement. OPEN_CHECK: Orders that have not yet received any payment. These are open tabs or orders still awaiting payment from the customer. NOTE: By default, this cube includes orders in ALL check states. Use the 'closed_checks' segment for standard sales reporting to match the behavior of Sales Summary reports.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.sale_timestamp
dimensions.title: Voids Sale Timestamp
dimensions.description: Sale timestamp, roughly based on cash accounting. Set to the time the final payments were received.

meta.label: Sale timestamp
meta.tooltip: Date and time when the transaction occurred.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_reporting_timestamp
dimensions.title: Voids Local Reporting Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_revenue_recognition_timestamp
dimensions.title: Voids Local Revenue Recognition Timestamp
dimensions.description: Full timestamp, where the UTC value of this timestamp represents the same time in the location's local timezone. Note - if using this dimension, you must not include a query timezone (or explicitly set it to UTC). If you need to specify a query timezone, you can use local_reporting_timestamp_str instead, or the individual local time dimensions (local_date, local_hour, local_minute, etc.) instead, as they provide more flexible grouping and filtering aligned with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.reporting_day
dimensions.title: Voids Reporting Day
dimensions.description: Seller reporting day for this row's revenue recognition timestamp, represented as midnight on the local reporting date. This is calculated from local_revenue_recognition_timestamp after applying the seller's reporting day start offset in minutes. Use this dimension for daily or coarser filtering and grouping only; for sub-daily aggregations or time frames, use local_revenue_recognition_timestamp instead. This field is still in development and is not ready for production use.

meta.label: Reporting day
meta.tooltip: Seller reporting day for the revenue recognition timestamp. Use for daily or coarser reporting; use local revenue recognition timestamp for sub-daily analysis.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_date
dimensions.title: Voids Local Date
dimensions.description: Date in YYYY-MM-dd format in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for date-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_day_of_week
dimensions.title: Voids Local Day of Week
dimensions.description: Day of week name in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for day-of-week analysis as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_month
dimensions.title: Voids Local Month
dimensions.description: Month (1-12) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for month-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_year
dimensions.title: Voids Local Year
dimensions.description: Year (YYYY) in the location's local timezone. This should be the preferred dimension for year-based filtering and grouping as it aligns with the business's operating hours. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.

dimensions.name: VoidsReport.local_hour
dimensions.title: Voids Local Hour
dimensions.description: Hour of day (0-23) in the location's local timezone. Use this dimension for hour-of-day questions such as busiest hour, peak time of day, morning/afternoon/evening analysis, or grouping across many dates by local clock hour. Do not use DATE_TRUNC(..., 'hour') or timestamp granularity hour for those questions, because that creates separate buckets for each date-hour instead of grouping all 9 AM rows together. This field is based on the seller's configured cash vs accrual revenue recognition setting and is still in development and not ready for production use.
